source.summary: Workstation experiencing thermal shutdowns and 0 RPM fan telemetry after BIOS firmware rollout
source.description:
Issue summary:
Several corporate developer laptops started reporting fan RPM as 0 in telemetry and have experienced thermal throttling or unexpected shutdowns after the March 8th BIOS firmware push. Affected devices are primarily Lenovo ThinkPad fleet that received the v1.2.3 BIOS rollback/upgrade during nightly autopilot/intune maintenance window.

Impact:
- Multiple engineers reported sudden shutdowns while compiling or running local model-serving containers.
- Telemetry shows CPU package temps > 95C and fan RPM 0 prior to shutdown.
- A subset of devices are recoverable by power-cycling; others require manual reseating of fan cable or BIOS re-flash.

Environment:
- corp-laptop | us-west region endpoints registered in Intune
- OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 22H2 with latest cumulative updates
- Endpoint management: Intune + Autopilot + custom vendor BIOS automation job
source.steps_to_reproduce:
1) Receive BIOS v1.2.3 via vendor auto-upgrade orchestrated through Intune.
2) Reboot as prompted to apply BIOS update.
3) Boot to Windows, run synthetic CPU load (e.g., Prime95 or compile job).
4) Observe fan telemetry via Company Telemetry Agent and Windows thermal events.
source.observed_logs:
- Company Telemetry Agent: fan_rpm=0, cpu_package_temp=98C, thermal_event=critical at 2026-03-09T02:14:33Z
- Windows Event Log: Kernel-Power ID 41 at 2026-03-09T02:14:34Z
- Intune device action log: BIOS update completed for machine GUID abcd-1234 at 2026-03-09T01:58:10Z
- Vendor BIOS logs (where available): FW_UPDATE_OK, but fan controller init returned error code 0xA5 on devices 1002,1007
source.investigation_notes:
- First triage (03/09): Confirmed correlation between install window and spike in thermal incidents. Many affected devices show firmware v1.2.3.
- Hypothesis A: BIOS v1.2.3 has a regression in fan controller initialization causing the embedded controller (EC) to not spin fans under Windows ACPI calls.
- Hypothesis B: The vendor packaging script accidentally toggled an EC config bit during provisioning job causing fans to be disabled until manual EC reset.
- Collected devices into secure bench. On two devices reseating fan connector returned fan RPM to normal; on three devices re-flashing BIOS v1.2.1 (previous) restored expected behavior.
- Telemetry team confirmed gradients: pre-update fan RPMs normal; immediately post-update many report 0.
- Cross-referenced with vendor forum: single report from partner company with similar symptoms after same BIOS build.
- Escalated to vendor support (case# VEND-7782) and Eng for triage. Created blocked test matrix for imaging pipeline to exclude BIOS v1.2.3 until vendor confirms fix.
source.temp_workaround:
- Instruct impacted users to power cycle and avoid heavy CPU loads.
- For machines on-site: MCS bench will reseat fan connector and re-flash BIOS back to v1.2.1 where possible.
- Disable automatic BIOS rollout job in Intune for the vendor SKU until vendor patch is validated.
- Adjust monitoring alert thresholds to detect sudden rise in 'fan_rpm==0' counts and proactively quarantine devices from build pools.
source.resolution:
- 2026-03-12: Vendor released BIOS v1.2.4 marked as hotfix addressing EC fan init regression (vendor case VEND-7782).
- Validation: Bench devices re-flashed to v1.2.4 show normal fan RPM under synthetic load; telemetry returns to baseline.
- Deployment plan: Staged rollout to remaining fleet with canary of 20 devices, monitoring for 48 hours, then roll to broader population.
- Follow-ups: Update runbook for BIOS vendor rollouts to include post-update fan sanity check, add pre-flight canary for firmware jobs, and add an automated rollback tag for updates that trigger >=3 thermal events within 1 hour.
comments: 2026-03-09 Priya Natarajan: Filed after two devs reported sudden shutdowns mid-compile. Captured telemetry and device GUIDs. Escalating to hardware bench.

2026-03-09 Miguel Alvarez: Reproduced on bench. Fan reported 0 RPM after update; reseating fan cable temporarily restores. Suspect EC initialization regression. Collecting vendor logs and device serials for vendor case.
2026-03-10 Vendor Support (VEND-7782): Acknowledged regression in v1.2.3 EC init routine. Working on hotfix. Requested sample dumps from affected devices.
2026-03-11 Telemetry Team: Dashboards updated to include 'fan_rpm==0' alert and quarantine rule. Canary set to 20 devices for v1.2.4 rollout.
2026-03-12 Miguel Alvarez: Applied v1.2.4 to 5 bench units; CPU stress test passed; scheduling canary rollout. Will update this ticket after 48h canary window.
2026-03-12 Priya Natarajan: Notified engineering teams via #internal-support and asked impacted engineers to hold off heavy builds until canary clears.

source.summary: New-hire onboarding: data-visualization console access stuck due to identity crosswalk mapping mismatch
source.description: Issue summary: A recently hired PM cannot access the internal DataViz console after completing SSO and SCIM provisioning. The user record appears in Okta and SCIM logs, but the console returns 403 with a message 'no roles assigned'. Impact: New hire onboarding blocked (cannot run dashboards or join pairing session). This is delaying a planned sync meeting tomorrow with the product team and is time-sensitive. Environment: Production (us-west), internal console behind identity-proxy, SCIM ingest pipeline v2.2.2. Steps to reproduce: 1) Invite new user via HR-provisioner -> Okta created profile. 2) Auto-provisioner runs SCIM PATCH to identity service. 3) User signs in via Okta SSO and is authenticated, but the console shows 403 and the support-runbook 'check-user-roles' returns empty role list. Observed logs: SCIM PATCH succeeded (200). Okta event shows user assigned to group "prod-dataviz-users". Identity-proxy log shows upstream call to authz service returned 204 NoContent. The console auth middleware logs: "role_lookup: 0 roles for subject=okta|12345-abcd". Hypothesis: crosswalk mapping between Okta group displayName and internal role key is failing due to a legacy transform that strips punctuation and lowercases; some groups created by IT include a trailing non-ASCII hyphen which is not normalized and therefore fails match in the role catalog.
steps_to_reproduce: Create Okta group with non-ASCII hyphen in displayName
Assign user to that Okta group
Allow SCIM provisioning to run and then attempt SSO login to console
logs: scim-provisioner: PATCH /Users/732 -> 200 (2026-03-09T18:12:03Z)
okta: event=group.assignment user=ava.test@redwood.com group=prod-dataviz\u2013team (note: U+2013 EN DASH present)
authz-service: GET /roles?group=prod-dataviz--team -> 404 (no mapping)
console: role_lookup: 0 roles for subject=okta|7321-abcd
investigation_notes: 03/10 09:45 - Support triage: confirmed user exists in Okta and SCIM; console role lookup returned empty list
03/10 11:30 - Pulled role-catalog dump from authz-service; entries contain 'prod-dataviz-team' (ASCII hyphen) but not the en-dash variant
03/10 13:10 - Replayed provisioning PATCH locally with displayName normalized to ASCII hyphen; role mapping succeeded and console access granted in staging replay

03/11 08:20 - Reviewed transformation code in identity-proxy: uses python unicodedata.normalize('NFKC') but then strips '-' only for ASCII. Non-ASCII hyphen characters pass through unchanged, so lookup key does not match catalog.
comments: Support (Ava Chen) 2026-03-10: Initial report from hiring manager — new PM cannot access DataViz; confirmed 403 with 'no roles assigned'
SRE (Devon Patel) 2026-03-10: Reproduced in a staging replay. Logging indicates mismatch in group name normalization. Will attach role-catalog snapshot
IAM Eng (Priya Nair) 2026-03-11: Asked for sample Okta group displayName with hex for the dash. Provided: 'prod-dataviz\u2013team' (U+2013 EN DASH). Suspect normalization bug in provisioner pipeline.
Support (Ava Chen) 2026-03-11: Customer-facing note prepared for hiring manager with workaround: manual role assignment until fixed.
IAM Eng (Priya Nair) 2026-03-12: Confirmed fix prepared: normalize group displayName using unicode NFKD + replace any dash-like chars with ASCII hyphen before lookup. Preparing PR and rollout plan.
source.root_cause: Role mapping relies on exact-match keys derived from Okta group displayName. Some Okta groups created by IT contained non-ASCII dash-like characters (e.g., en-dash U+2013). The SCIM/provisioner and identity-proxy pipeline did not fully normalize these variants to the canonical ASCII hyphen, so lookup against the role catalog failed, returning no roles.
source.resolution: Temporary workaround: manually assign the 'dataviz-viewer' role to the affected user in the admin console (documented in the support note sent to the hiring manager). Permanent fix deployed: PR #1724 normalizes incoming group displayName by applying unicode NFKD normalization, mapping common dash-like characters (U+2013, U+2014, U+2212) to ASCII hyphen, then lowercasing and collapsing whitespace before performing role lookup. The fix was rolled out to identity-proxy and provisioner v2.2.3 on 2026-03-12 in us-west with canary at 10% then 100% after 30 minutes. Post-deploy verification: recreating the original user flow now yields correct role lookup and console access.
postmortem_follow_up: Add unit tests for dash-like character normalization in identity-proxy and scim-provisioner
Run a catalog-scan job to detect existing Okta groups with non-ASCII punctuation and create a remediation plan (automation or guided IT cleanup)
Add telemetry alert: spike in 'role_lookup: 0 roles' per new-user provisioning events to catch regressions
Document workaround and update onboarding runbook to include group name normalization guidance for IT

source.summary: Runner secret-cache livelock during bulk key rewrap blocks release cutover
source.description:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner credentials, a subset of runners entered a secret-cache livelock where vault-sync repeatedly refreshed secrets but the runner-provisioner refused to mark nodes healthy. This prevented the release cutover job from acquiring the required pool of labeled runners and blocked the production deploy window.

Impact:
- Release cutover delayed for two release pipelines (nightly + hotfix).
- ~18% of ephemeral runner fleet in us-west-2 and 12% in eu-central-1 continuously flapped between REGISTERED and UNHEALTHY, failing job assignment.
- Some long-running jobs were evicted and re-queued, creating billing churn.

Environment:
- prod account (redwood-prod) running 2026-03-10 KMS rewrap job initiated from operations-rotate-key pipeline.
- Runner image v2026.02.1, runner-provisioner v1.14.0, vault-sync v0.9.3.
- Autoscaler policy: conservative-surge with max-unavailable 10%.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Triggered bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner service account keys across regions.
2) vault-sync pushed new wrapped secrets and incremented secret-version labels on k8s Secret objects.
3) Runner pods attempted to fetch new secret; cache layer (runner-provisioner cache) attempted a soft-refresh but observed nonce mismatch with authz-cache table.
4) Provisioner flagged pod as unhealthy and restarted the token renewal flow. vault-sync simultaneously rewrapped again due to perceived failure, renewing secret-version and causing the runner to see a new version mid-refresh.
5) Loop repeated until operator intervention.

Logs / Observations:
- Vault-sync logs (us-west-2): many 2026-03-10T22:14:xx entries "secret rewrap: success version=222->223" followed quickly by retries for same pod IDs.
- Runner-provisioner logs: repeated "cache nonce mismatch, dropping token refresh" followed by "marking runner UNHEALTHY: token-refresh-failed" for same container IDs.
- Authz-cache (redis) had transient high CPU and eviction spikes coincident with the rewrap window.
- Scheduler metrics showed job-assign latency spike to 4.1s (baseline 0.6s) and pending-job queue growth.

Investigation notes:
- Hypothesis: concurrent rewraps + eventual consistency of authz-cache caused the provisioner to observe mixed secret-versions across components and treat that as compromise, triggering aggressive eviction.
- Vault-sync retry/backoff is too eager when secrets appear outdated; it will rewrap again for a pod perceived as failed, which escalates version churn.
- The provisioner cache validates both secret-version and nonce; if versions do not align within a short window, it refuses to accept the token and evicts. The short acceptance window is too tight for our multi-region rewrap choreography.
- Related: similar pattern to INT-30542 (runner deregistered during credentials rotation) but here it manifests as livelock due to compounded rewrap retries across components.

Temporary workaround implemented:
- Paused the rolling rewrap in eu-central-1 and set vault-sync to single-threaded mode for us-west-2.
- Increased provisioner token acceptance window (config toggle) from 5s to 45s as an emergency mitigation to allow reconciliation.
- Manually reconciled ~120 affected pods by forcing a single rewrap and restarting runner-provisioner for the affected node pool.

Resolution plan (short-term):
- Stabilize by rolling a hotfix to vault-sync to add optimistic backoff and a per-pod rewrap gate so we don't rewrap the same pod while a previous refresh is in-flight. Target: vault-sync v0.9.4.
- Roll hotfix to runner-provisioner to make cache-version acceptance window configurable and to add a one-time idempotency token check to tolerate brief version skew. Target: v1.14.1.
- Increase authz-cache resource requests and add Redis persistence tuning to avoid eviction during rotation windows.
- Re-run controlled rotation with feature flags enabling single-region stagger and observability hooks.

Follow-ups and postmortem items:
- Postmortem to be authored once stable with timeline, blame-free root cause, and remediation owner assignments.
- Add automated test that simulates concurrent rewrap + cache skew to CI test harness.
- Update the Release Cutover Checklist to include a secret-rotation readiness gate.
- Consider orchestration change: central coordinator that stages rewraps per runner label instead of region-wide parallel rewraps.
comments: 2026-03-10 23:05 - Rajiv Menon (SRE): Initial triage — observed cluster-wide rewrap churn and eviction pattern. Paused rotation in eu-central-1.
2026-03-11 01:12 - Hannah Li (Runner Team): Reproducer hypothesis: provisioner rejects when nonce != cached_nonce AND secret-version mismatch. Investigating acceptance-window tweak.
2026-03-11 05:40 - Mateo Alvarez (Assignee): Emergency mitigation applied: increased acceptance window, restarted provisioner for impacted pools. Monitoring shows pending queue dropping.
2026-03-12 09:20 - Priya Rao (Security): Confirmed no credential exfiltration indicators. Rotation deemed safe; recommend adding audit events for rewrap actions.
2026-03-12 14:02 - Evelyn Brooks (Reporter): Scheduling controlled rewrap for 2026-03-14 02:00 UTC with staggered regions and on-call SRE present.
source.resolution_notes: Hotfixes planned: vault-sync v0.9.4 (optimistic backoff + per-pod gate) and runner-provisioner v1.14.1 (configurable acceptance window + idempotency token). Authz-cache tuned and more conservative rotation orchestration to be adopted. Postmortem owner: Mateo Alvarez. Estimated regression window closed after incremental rollouts.
next_steps: Merge vault-sync PR with per-pod gate and backoff
Roll runner-provisioner config change and bake in staging
Re-run secret rotation with single-region stagger and SLOs monitored
Create postmortem and update runbooks

source.summary: Okta claim encoding collision prevents RBAC provisioning for internal ops console
source.summary_detail: Internal users intermittently receive 403 when accessing the Ops Console even though Okta group membership shows correct entitlements. Issue started after a recent Okta connector upgrade that changed claim serialization for groups with non-ASCII characters.
source.description:
Issue summary:
Users are denied access to the Ops Console with a 403 RBAC error even though their Okta profiles include the expected groups. The denial appears tied to group claim encoding changes introduced by last week's Okta connector upgrade (v2.7.1).

Impact:
Multiple engineering and SRE users cannot access runbook/incident tooling. This blocks oncall rotations and delays incident response. Affected users are primarily in us-west and include contractors and full-time staff.

Observed behavior:
- Users report 403 when opening /console/runbooks (UI) and 403 from CLI tooling that hits the same token-exchange endpoint.
- Okta shows group membership correctly (group names include em-dash and accented characters for some internal teams).
- Our identity proxy logs show group claim payloads with mixed percent-encoding and UTF-8 bytes, which the RBAC service fails to normalize.

Expected behavior:
Group claims should be normalized to our internal canonical format and RBAC grants applied, so users with the correct groups receive the expected console roles.

Steps to reproduce (observed with affected user):
1) User signs in via Okta SSO and hits /auth/callback.
2) Identity proxy receives id_token with claim groups: ["eng—platform", "oncall-équipe"] where the em-dash and accented e are present.
3) After connector upgrade, some tokens contain percent-encoded bytes like "eng%EF%80%81-platform" or a mix of bytes and Unicode characters.
4) RBAC mapping stage performs a literal string lookup against canonical group keys and does not match, resulting in a 403.

Relevant logs and artifacts:
- Identity proxy request sample (redacted) available in link: slack://channel/#support?thread=G12345 (private logs pinned).
- Auth service logs: auth-proxy@us-west-02:2026-03-10T09:12:23Z - WARN group_claim_parse failed for user u-8743 (payload contains invalid percent-encoding).
- GitHub issue from connector vendor: https://github.com/okta/okta-connector/issues/4183 (related encoding change).
- Confluence runbook draft: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/okta-claim-normalization (internal).

Investigation notes:
- Hypothesis 1: The v2.7.1 connector changed how multi-byte characters are serialized into group claims, moving from pure UTF-8 to a percent-encoded fallback for certain legacy Okta org profiles.
- Hypothesis 2: The identity proxy previously accepted both percent-encoded and UTF-8 forms and normalized them; a recent auth-proxy upgrade (2026-03-08) added stricter validation that rejects mixed encodings.
- Quick test: Reproduced by creating test user with group name containing em-dash (—) and forced connector to return percent-encoded sequence; identity proxy returned a parsing error and RBAC lookup failed.
- Affected surface: UI auth flow, CLI token-exchange, and service-to-service token refresh where group claims are evaluated.

Mitigation attempted so far:
- Rolled back auth-proxy change on one canary host to re-enable tolerant parsing; that host successfully allowed previously blocked user to authenticate and receive roles.
- Workaround for affected users: Use the legacy session endpoint (session-legacy.redwood.ai) which hits the older proxy path that still normalizes claims. This is not sustainable.

Next steps / proposed fix:
1) Short-term: Patch auth-proxy to implement a normalization pass that percent-decodes group claim strings then applies Unicode NFC normalization before RBAC lookup. Target ETA: 2026-03-13 EOD for hotfix.
2) Medium-term: Add automated unit tests and fuzzing for claim encodings in identity-proxy repo.
3) Long-term: Coordinate with Okta connector maintainers to ensure consistent serialization and add integration test coverage.

Related items:
- linked_issues: ["INT-202999", "INT-4251"]
  (INT-202999 is an earlier Okta claim mismatch ticket; INT-4251 references group sync dashboard access issue.)

Resolution notes (planned):
- Hotfix auth-proxy on fleet will be rolled out via canary -> 25% -> 100% with monitoring on auth-errors and RBAC-deny rates.
- Post-deploy, ask impacted users to re-login and confirm access.

Rolled back strict parsing on single canary host
Created temp workaround route to legacy session endpoint

title: Okta claim encoding collision prevents RBAC provisioning for internal ops console
preview:
Issue summary:
Users are denied access to the Ops Console with a 403 RBAC error even though their Okta profiles include the expected groups. The denial appears tied to group claim encoding changes introduced by last week's Okta connector upgrade (v2.7.1).

Impact:
Multiple engineering and SRE users cannot access runbook/incident tooling. This blocks oncall rotations and delays incident response. Affected users are primarily in us-west and include contractors and full-time staff.

Observed behavior:
- Users report 403 when opening /console/runbooks (UI) and 403 from CLI tooling that hits the same token-exchange endpoint.
- Okta shows group membership correctly (group names include em-dash and accented characters for some internal teams).
- Our identity proxy logs show group claim payloads with mixed percent-encoding and UTF-8 bytes, which the RBAC service fails to normalize.

Expected behavior:
Group claims should be normalized to our internal canonical format and RBAC grants applied, so users with the correct groups receive the expected console roles.

Steps to reproduce (observed with affected user):
1) User signs in via Okta SSO and hits /auth/callback.
2) Identity proxy receives id_token with claim groups: [\"eng—platform\", \"oncall-équipe\"] where the em-dash and accented e are present.
3) After connector upgrade, some tokens contain percent-encoded bytes like \"eng%EF%80%81-platform\" or a mix of bytes and Unicode characters.
4) RBAC mapping stage performs a literal string lookup against canonical group keys and does not match, resulting in a 403.

Relevant logs and artifacts:
- Identity proxy request sample (redacted) available in link: slack://channel/#support?thread=G12345 (private logs pinned).
- Auth service logs: auth-proxy@us-west-02:2026-03-10T09:12:23Z - WARN group_claim_parse failed for user u-8743 (payload contains invalid percent-encoding).
- GitHub issue from connector vendor: https://github.com/okta/okta-connector/issues/4183 (related encoding change).
- Confluence runbook draft: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/okta-claim-normalization (internal).

Investigation notes:
- Hypothesis 1: The v2.7.1 connector changed how multi-byte characters are serialized into group claims, moving from pure UTF-8 to a percent-encoded fallback for certain legacy Okta org profiles.
- Hypothesis 2: The identity proxy previously accepted both percent-encoded and UTF-8 forms and normalized them; a recent auth-proxy upgrade (2026-03-08) added stricter validation that rejects mixed encodings.
- Quick test: Reproduced by creating test user with group name containing em-dash (—) and forced connector to return percent-encoded sequence; identity proxy returned a parsing error and RBAC lookup failed.
- Affected surface: UI auth flow, CLI token-exchange, and service-to-service token refresh where group claims are evaluated.

Mitigation attempted so far:
- Rolled back auth-proxy change on one canary host to re-enable tolerant parsing; that host successfully allowed previously blocked user to authenticate and receive roles.
- Workaround for affected users: Use the legacy session endpoint (session-legacy.redwood.ai) which hits the older proxy path that still normalizes claims. This is not sustainable.

Next steps / proposed fix:
1) Short-term: Patch auth-proxy to implement a normalization pass that percent-decodes group claim strings then applies Unicode NFC normalization before RBAC lookup. Target ETA: 2026-03-13 EOD for hotfix.
2) Medium-term: Add automated unit tests and fuzzing for claim encodings in identity-proxy repo.
3) Long-term: Coordinate with Okta connector maintainers to ensure consistent serialization and add integration test coverage.

Related items:
- linked_issues: [\"INT-202999\", \"INT-4251\"]
  (INT-202999 is an earlier Okta claim mismatch ticket; INT-4251 references group sync dashboard access issue.)

Resolution notes (planned):
- Hotfix auth-proxy on fleet will be rolled out via canary -> 25% -> 100% with monitoring on auth-errors and RBAC-deny rates.
- Post-deploy, ask impacted users to re-login and confirm access.

source.summary: Routing escalation matrix drift affecting SLA-based routing for multi-tenant support flows
source.description:
Issue summary:
Support tooling routing rules have diverged from the canonical SLA escalation matrix for a subset of multi-tenant routes. Some P1 routes for Dedicated-tier customers are being escalated to L2 on the wrong schedule (30m instead of 15m), and a few self-serve routes are hitting manual-intervention macros unexpectedly.

Impact:
- Potential customer-visible violation of escalation SLA for ~4 Dedicated customers over the last 48h.
- Increased mean time to engage for urgent tickets; reported by on-call as customer-impacting but intermittent.
- Reporting shows mismatched counts between routing-engine logs and support-console SLA buckets.

Scope / affected areas:
- Routing-engine rule set for multi-tenant routes generated by sla-config-service v1.9.1
- Support console macros for escalation (templates/escalate/*)
- Reporting pipeline aggregations for SLA breach counts

Initial hypothesis:
- A config templating regression in sla-config-service produced stale escalation intervals for routes created via the bulk-importer job run 2026-03-08.
- A subsequent partial rollback of config templates may have left a hybrid state: some routes referencing legacy policy IDs while the console expects the new policy schema.

Steps to reproduce (investigator notes):
1) Query routing-engine for routes created between 2026-03-07 and 2026-03-09 with tag:multi-tenant=true and customer_tier=dedicated.
2) Compare escalation.interval and escalation.policy_id between returned entries and SLA canonical table from Confluence.
3) Trigger a synthetic ticket against a sample route and observe escalation timing via support-console metrics.

Observed logs / telemetry pointers:
- routing-engine/error.log contains repeated WARN: "policy_lookup_fallback: policy_id 0x1f not found, using legacy_timing" (2026-03-09 03:12:07 UTC).
- sla-config-service audit trail shows bulk-import job id BI-20260308 completed with 1,246 updated routes.
- support-console and reporting-pipeline counts diverge by ~5-8% for P1 escalation events in us-east for 2026-03-09.

Immediate impact mitigation (temporary):
- Support on-call asked to manually reassign affected tickets to L2 if escalation timing appears delayed.
- Rolled forward a guard rule in routing-engine to enforce minimum escalation interval of 15m for any route with tier=dedicated (hotfix applied 2026-03-10 05:40 UTC).

Requested next steps:
- Full diff of sla-config templates from v1.9.0 -> v1.9.1 and the bulk-importer transformations.
- Re-run the bulk-import job in dry-run against a staging snapshot and validate policy_id mapping logic.
- Reconcile reporting counts after authoritative fix and backfill any missed escalations if needed.
- Postmortem and a small automation: validation job that flags routes where escalation.interval != canonical_interval.
source.investigation_notes:
2026-03-10: Diego ran targeted queries and confirmed ~42 routes have legacy timing values. Audit of bulk-importer shows a missing mapping for policy alias 'dedicated-v1' introduced in v1.9.1. The guard rule mitigated immediate customer impact but is a stopgap.
2026-03-11: Reproduced in staging by re-running importer on a subset; bug in template renderer (missing conditional) caused policy_id to be omitted for routes without a 'region' attribute.
Evidence: commit 9f3b7c in ops-config altered template 'escalation.tpl' and removed the fallback branch.
comments: Ava Chen (Support): Logged after on-call report that two Dedicated customers reported delayed escalation. Added initial telemetry pointers and created ticket.
Diego Morales (SRE): Confirmed WARN lines in routing-engine logs. Applied hotfix to enforce minimum 15m escalation for Dedicated tier. Investigating bulk-importer artifacts.
Maya Patel (Tooling): Noted that we deployed sla-config-service v1.9.1 on 2026-03-08 which included the new templating change. I'll reproduce locally and submit PR if confirmed.
Diego Morales (SRE): Reproduced missing policy_id in staging when 'region' tag absent. Creating PR to restore fallback and adding unit tests for template edge cases (see PR #219).
Maya Patel (Tooling): PR #219 opened; request review from infra team. Also proposing a nightly validation job to detect future drift (consequence: small pipeline job + alert).
follow_up_tasks: ops-config: add unit test and restore fallback in escalation.tpl (PR 219)
Run reconciliation job to normalize policy_id for affected routes (target 2026-03-13)
Add nightly drift validator to CI (create INT follow-up ticket for automation)

source.summary: Machine cert CRL/OCSP fetch failure causes VPN tunnel collapse and loss of internal DNS suffixes
source.description:
Issue summary: After a scheduled corporate machine certificate rotation, a subset of macOS and Windows hosts fail to establish the zero-trust VPN agent. Impact: Affected hosts lose corporate DNS suffix resolution (internal.*), causing service access failures (k8s API, internal dashboards, git over https). Scope: ~120 hosts in us-east over the last 24h. Pattern: failures cluster around clients that were asleep or on flaky Wi-Fi during cert push.

Observed behavior: Zero-trust agent shows cert validation errors; system logs show CRL/OCSP fetch timeouts; VPN client repeatedly tears down and rebinds network interfaces which clears DNS suffixes. Expected behavior: Agent should accept new machine certs or gracefully retry CRL/OCSP without dropping tunnel/DNS on transient fetch failures.

Customer/Business impact: Internal developer productivity affected; CI jobs hitting internal artifact registries failing; small number of oncall SREs unable to access internal consoles.
source.impact: ~120 hosts intermittently unable to reach internal services; developer productivity and CI impacted; manual reboots or reconnects restore connectivity for most machines.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Simulate machine cert rotation by provisioning new machine cert via internal PKI and revoking old cert from CA
2) Put client to sleep and wake after cert distribution completes (simulates laptop asleep during rotation)
3) Observe zero-trust agent attempting OCSP/CRL lookup for new cert and timing out on flaky Wi-Fi
4) Observe VPN client session tear down and system DNS suffix being cleared
5) Attempt to resolve internal hostnames (internal.k8s, vcs.redwood.local) and observe NXDOMAIN or search-suffix-less resolution failures
source.observed_behavior: Zero-trust agent logs: 'certificate validation failed: OCSP responder unreachable' followed by rapid vpn-client reconnects. Network logs show DHCP renews and DNS suffix list losing 'corp.redwood.internal' entry. Rolling back to previous cert state (where possible) restores stable sessions.
source.expected_behavior: Agent should queue OCSP/CRL retries without forcing immediate tunnel teardown or clearing DNS search suffix list on transient fetch failures.
logs: Mar 10 09:12:34 host-23 zero-trust-agent[421]: cert_validation: OCSP lookup timeout for serial 0x4b3f (responder 10.11.5.3)
Mar 10 09:12:35 host-23 vpn-client[212]: session: graceful tear down triggered due to cert validation error
Mar 10 09:12:36 host-23 systemd-networkd[88]: link: DNS search domains cleared
Mar 10 09:13:02 host-77 zero-trust-agent[421]: cert_validation: fetched CRL but signature check failed (cache corrupted?)
Mar 10 09:14:10 dhcp-client[99]: received new domain-search: [ ] (previous: [corp.redwood.internal])
investigation_notes: Initial triage: errors align with certificate rotation window (2026-03-10 02:00 UTC).
Correlation: affected hosts show high packet loss on Wi-Fi APs BSSID:88:44:12:* and 40% were on older zero-trust agent 2.7.1 (known to aggressively teardown on cert errors).
Reproducer: able to reproduce locally by blocking outbound 10.11.5.3:80 (OCSP) from client and forcing cert revalidation — agent tears down VPN after 2 consecutive OCSP timeouts.
Hypothesis A: agent aggressive failure policy triggers teardown when OCSP unreachable; should be tolerant for transient network.
Hypothesis B: DHCP client on rebind resets DNS search path when VPN interface flaps; race condition between vpn-client and network service causes suffix removal.
Telemetry: VPN connection churn correlated with DNS suffix loss events in net-metrics graph (link: https://grafana.redwood/internal/d/abcd/net-metrics?var=region=us-east).
Cross-ref: INT-14762 (cert rotation impacting VPN onboarding) appears related; this incident exhibits stronger OCSP fetch timeouts and DNS suffix loss rather than onboarding failures.
Agent versions: 2.7.1 (aggressive), 2.8.0-rc (contains backoff change) — only 2.7.1 hosts show deterministic failures.
comments: Ava Chen (Reporter) [2026-03-10 10:05]: Noticed post-rotation spike in VPN disconnects and multiple developers reporting NXDOMAIN for internal services. Rebooting clients fixes temporarily.
SRE Triage (Maya Patel) [2026-03-10 11:12]: Reproduced with OCSP block. Marking P1. Adding Liam to investigate agent behavior and DHCP interaction.

Liam O'Connor (Assignee) [2026-03-10 12:40]: Captured packet dump from affected host and verified repeated HTTP GETs to OCSP responder 10.11.5.3, then vpn-client issues SIGHUP to network stack. Dump uploaded to S3: s3://redwood-support/INT-221403/ocsp-pcap.pcap

Zero-trust eng (Ravi Singh) [2026-03-11 09:22]: Confirmed agent 2.7.1 will mark cert as invalid after 2 timeouts and triggers 'rekey-fail' path. 2.8.0-rc defers teardown for up to 5 retries. Need rollback plan or hotfix to increase retry/backoff for 2.7.x.

Network eng (Jocelyn Park) [2026-03-11 11:03]: Observed DHCP client on Windows machines resets 'DNS Suffix Search List' when vpn interface is removed; behaviour differs across OS versions. Suggest gating suffix persistence to VPN service if possible.

Liam O'Connor [2026-03-12 08:45]: Working on mitigations: (1) push 2.8.0-rc to Canary pool (50 hosts), (2) add whitelist to firewall to allow OCSP responder from Wi-Fi captive networks, (3) patch DHCP hook to avoid wiping search domains during brief vpn interface down.
next_steps: 1) Canary rollout 2.8.0-rc to 50 affected hosts and monitor for disconnect rate reduction (owner: Liam).
2) Implement DHCP hook patch to preserve DNS suffix list on transient interface teardown (owner: Jocelyn).
3) Add firewall rule to allow OCSP/CRL responder access from corporate Wi-Fi APs during rotation window (owner: NetOps).
4) Postmortem once mitigations validated and resume full rotation with staggered batches to reduce blast radius.
resolution_notes: In progress — mitigations planned. Canary started 2026-03-12 09:00 UTC.
If Canary shows >50% reduction in DNS-loss events after 24h, schedule phased rollout and mark resolved.

source.summary: EDI-imported supplier invoice lost cost-center attributes on multi-line invoices; posted to default project
source.description: Issue summary: A supplier invoice submitted via EDI (X12 810) with three invoice lines was posted in the finance ledger with no cost-center/project attributes on any lines; the invoice header defaulted to Project 0000. Impact: misallocation of expenses for two engineering projects (ENG-Alpha, ENG-Beta) and potential incorrect vendor payment amounts due to downstream automated chargebacks. This occurred for one vendor (Sable Components) today after an automated batch run. The invoice was accepted by the EDI ingestion service but the AP posting in the ERP dropped per-line cost-center tags when the invoice contained 3+ lines that reference PO line splits.
source.impact: Two active projects show unexpected expense; month-end P&L will be impacted if not reclassified. Payment to vendor scheduled tomorrow; if paid without fix, we will need manual journal entries and may disrupt PM workflows. Current exposure: ~USD 28,400 across 3 GL codes.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Receive EDI 810 file from vendor (batch 2026-03-10-batch17)
2) AP-EDI service transforms X12 -> internal invoice JSON via mapping template edi-810-v2
3) ETL posts invoice lines to finance-system API endpoint /invoices/create
4) Invoice accepted; finance-system UI shows header project defaulted to 0000 and line-level cost_center empty
5) Observe ledger entries created against default project and clearing account instead of per-line project GLs
source.expected_behavior: Per-line cost center attributes from the EDI (CC tag) should be preserved and mapped to the corresponding invoice line objects in the finance system; header project should remain blank unless vendor provided a header-level project.
source.actual_behavior: All line-level CC attributes stripped during transform; finance-system created invoice with header project set to default '0000' and no line-level project assignments.
investigation_notes: Checked EDI ingress logs (ingest-2026-03-10.log): transform completed successfully without recorded errors at 08:31:02 UTC.
Reviewed mapping template edi-810-v2: mapping path for per-line custom qualifier 'CC' is present but uses a conditional that appears to overwrite when more than 2 IT1 segments exist.
ETL payload delivered to finance-system (payload-id: pl-98177) shows invoice.lines array with 3 entries, but all lines have cost_center: null in JSON.

Finance-system audit shows a fallback: if any line.cost_center is null, system sets invoice.header.project = '0000' and clears per-line project fields (legacy safeguard introduced 2024-11 to handle partial mappings).
Hypothesis: mapping conditional for 'CC' qualifier uses a non-deterministic reducer which returns null when array length > 2 (regression introduced in mapping library upgrade to 1.9.0 on 2026-02-28).
Ran local unit test of mapping function against sample EDI: reproduces null cost_center when IT1 count >= 3.
Confirmed only invoices with multi-line EDI (>=3 IT1 segments) are affected; single- and two-line invoices ingest correctly.
logs: ingest-2026-03-10.log: [08:31:02] INFO Transform completed (file=batch17, template=edi-810-v2, duration=320ms)
etl-2026-03-10.log: [08:31:10] DEBUG POST /invoices/create payload-id=pl-98177 status=200
finance-api-2026-03-10.log: [08:31:12] WARN Missing line-level project fields; applying fallback '0000' for header project
comments: 2026-03-10 - Marcus Patel (AP): Noted payment scheduled tomorrow; requesting immediate hold on INV-8899. Will create a payment suspense for vendor until reclassification.
2026-03-10 - Engineering (SRE): Added triage label; mapping library 1.9.0 upgrade rollback considered. We need exact unit test failing cases.
2026-03-11 - Ava Chen (Reporter): Uploaded sample EDI file and payload pl-98177 to the ticket attachments. Requesting ETA for fix — month end close is next week.
2026-03-11 - Marcus Patel (AP): Workaround prepared: create manual journal to move lines to ENG-Alpha and ENG-Beta after payment, but prefer to correct source to avoid repeat.

2026-03-12 - Eng Lead (Priya Nair): Reproduced locally. Root cause narrowed to map.reduce() usage in edi-transformer v1.9.0; sequence order non-deterministic when mapping same qualifier across IT1 segments. Preparing PR to fix deterministic reduce behavior.
2026-03-12 - Marcus Patel (AP): Holding payment and informing vendor of delayed remittance pending fix.
source.temporary_workaround: AP to place invoice INV-8899 on payment hold and create manual reclassification journal post-payment. Finance will contact vendor to delay payment until mapping fix is deployed. For impacted historical invoices, run a one-off correction job using 'edi-reprocess' with forced per-line CC extraction.
fix_plan: 1) Revert edi-transformer to 1.8.7 on staging to validate behavior parity (short term rollback)
2) Merge PR#452: change reducer to deterministic concat and add explicit per-line qualifier parsing; add unit tests covering >2 IT1 segments
3) Deploy patched edi-transformer v1.9.1 to staging, run full regression, then deploy to prod during maintenance window (preferred: 2026-03-13 02:00-03:00 UTC)
4) Reprocess affected batch17 files with reprocess tool and verify finance ledger shows correct per-line project allocation
5) Create post-deploy monitoring alert for any invoice with line.count >=3 and any null cost_center in ingestion payload
source.resolution_notes: (Pending) PR created and in review. Short-term: payment held; manual journal prepared if vendor insists. Long-term: mapping fix scheduled for 2026-03-13 maintenance window. After deployment, run reprocess for batch17 and close ticket.

title: EDI-imported supplier invoice lost cost-center attributes on multi-line invoices; posted to default project

This occurred for one vendor (Sable Components) today after an automated batch run. source.summary: Intermittent assume-role failures due to mismatched session tag attribute encoding between GCP WIF and AWS STS
source.description: Summary: Jobs that bootstrap dedicated GPU provisioners via the auth-proxy intermittently fail to assume the target AWS role. Errors show AccessDenied: Not authorized to perform sts:AssumeRole or downstream permission checks fail when role session tags parsed by the provisioner are missing expected attributes. This started after last week's auth-proxy update that changed WIF-to-STS attribute normalization. Impact: Several Dedicated onboarding runs and internal automated repro jobs failed, blocking a small number of customer onboarding tasks and delaying a production scale test. Intermittent: ~10% of assume-role attempts in the last 48h failed.
source.impact: Dedicated provisioning jobs intermittently fail to assume AWS roles; blocks internal repros and delays a handful of customer onboards. Estimated affected jobs: ~12/day during peak.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Trigger the dedicated onboarding pipeline that uses GCP WIF->AWS role federation through auth-proxy
2) Observe the auth-proxy logs for request->response attribute mapping
3) In failing cases, the provisioner logs show downstream permission errors referencing missing 'env' or truncated 'role-session' attributes
4) Re-run with verbose STS debug: CloudTrail shows AssumeRole succeeded but session tags truncated/rewritten; provisioner denies due to absent tag guardrails
investigation_notes: Auth-proxy change on 2026-03-08 started normalizing multivalue attributes into a single JSON-encoded string; this appears to exceed AWS STS tag length in some flows
CloudTrail evidence: 90% of failed AssumeRole calls return 200 with AssumedRoleUser but the session tag list is shorter than expected (missing 'env' or 'team')

GCP audit logs: WIF token contained attributes 'environment=prod' and 'team=platform', auth-proxy forwarded 'attributes' as 'attrs:{"environment":"prod","team":"platform"}' string which STS interprets as a single tag 'attrs' instead of separate tags
Provisioner enforces tag-based RBAC: expects 'env' and 'team' top-level tags; when tags are absent permission engine rejects requests
Reproduced locally by issuing WIF with long attribute payload -> AWS STS drops tags beyond 64 characters per tag key/value limit

Hypothesis: two contributing factors — auth-proxy JSON-encoding of attributes and STS per-tag length/truncation behavior. Some attribute values combined in a single tag causing guardrail checks to fail.
workarounds_tried: 1) Temporary rollback of auth-proxy normalization to previous behavior — confirmed immediate reduction in failures in a small canary cluster
2) Short-term change in provisioner RBAC to accept 'attrs' combined tag (risk: weakens policy precision) — tested in staging, allowed repro jobs to proceed
3) Manual re-run of blocked onboards after rolling back auth-proxy in controlled window
source.proposed_fix: Primary fix: update auth-proxy attribute mapping to emit individual STS session tags per attribute (key->value) and ensure each tag respects AWS limits (max 128 characters for value, 128 for key). Add explicit truncation/validation and a feature-flag to opt-in. Secondary: harden provisioner to parse combined 'attrs' tag as JSON only after verifying tag presence, emit a telemetry metric when fallback parsing is used.
source.risk_and_rollout_notes: Change must be rolled out to auth-proxy instances in all regions. Rollout steps: (1) add validation and flag disabled by default; (2) enable in canary in eu-west-1 for 24h; (3) monitor CloudTrail and provisioner RBAC metrics; (4) progressively enable across us-east-1 and other regions. Provisioner RBAC fallback should be maintained until full rollforward confirmed. Backout: revert to previous auth-proxy image.
source.resolution: In progress — a patch branch has been opened that splits attributes into individual tags and enforces per-tag length limits. Canaries scheduled for eu-west-1 on 2026-03-12. Full rollout pending successful 24h canary. Temporary mitigation (auth-proxy rollback) reduced failures; provisioner fallback parsing remains as secondary safety net.
next_steps: Assign PR reviewer and merge small fix (auth-proxy) — owner: Aisha Patel
Schedule canary deployment (eu-west-1) and monitor for 24h — SRE oncall (Miguel Santos)
Update dedicated-provisioner to add metric 'session_tag_parsing.fallback' and a dashboard tile — owner: Platform Eng
Communicate to internal onboarding owners about short window where manual retries may be required
comments: Aisha Patel (2026-03-10): Found the auth-proxy normalization commit; starting a scoped fix branch to emit discrete tags.
Miguel Santos (2026-03-11): Can confirm rollback to previous auth-proxy image drops failure rate to <1% in canary cluster.
SRE Lead (2026-03-12): Please include CloudTrail event id sampling in the canary run so we can correlate per-request traces.
Ben Carter (2026-03-12): Added the provisioner RBAC fallback as a temporary mitigation; will be removed after full rollout.

source.summary: Provisioning stall after depot repair: kernel module signature enforcement blocks corporate NIC driver and prevents management check-in
source.description: Issue summary: Several Dell Latitude 7440 units returned from depot repair fail to fully provision after the post-repair image. The corporate NIC kernel module (corp-nic.ko) is present on disk but is not loaded due to kernel module signature enforcement. Because the signed driver is rejected, the system falls back to a generic NIC, which receives a DHCP address on an isolated vlan and cannot reach SCCM/Intune, causing provisioning/check-in to stall. Impact: devices remain in 'staged' state, cannot retrieve device policies or complete post-image configuration, delaying reprovisioning and increasing depot turnover time.
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Depot repair triggers replacement of mainboard and re-flash of UEFI/BIOS to latest vendor image
2) Technician reimages device with our standard depot image (image version 2026.02.14) which includes corp-nic module signed with our previous key
3) Reboot into UEFI Secure Boot: Secure Boot enabled by default on vendor image
4) Observe kernel messages: corp-nic.ko rejected (module_sig_enforce) and fallback driver binds
5) Device obtains network but on isolated vlan and cannot reach management endpoints; SCCM/Intune check-in fails and provisioning scripts time out
logs: dmesg excerpts from affected unit: 'module validation failed: signature and/or required key missing - corp-nic.ko',
journalctl -b: 'NetworkManager: bound fallback driver iwl_pci, corp-nic not loaded',
SCCM logs: 'Client failed to contact management point after 10 retries; error 0x80072efd'
investigation_notes: Initial hypothesis: depot image includes an older module signing key that doesn't match current MOK list used by Secure Boot on updated vendor firmware.
Reproduced on staging by enabling Secure Boot and installing corp-nic.ko signed with deprecated key: kernel rejects load with signature verification error.
Observed vendor BIOS update during repair stage resets/customizes UEFI key database causing our previously accepted key to be missing from MOKdb.
Workaround tested: enrolling the new signing key with mokutil (sudo mokutil --import newkey.der) and performing MOK enrollment allowed the module to load and SCCM check-in succeeded.
Alternative workaround validated: booting with module.sig_enforce=0 allows module load but is unacceptable as a permanent depot step due to security policy.
comments: 2026-03-10 09:18 - Ava Chen: Ticket opened after depot team reported 4/20 Dell 7440s failing final check-in. Attaching dmesg & SCCM logs.
2026-03-10 11:42 - Samir Patel: Confirmed kernel rejects corp-nic due to signature. Reproduced locally on staging image. Adding imaging and security owners.
2026-03-11 08:30 - Luis Moreno (Depot Lead): Noted vendor board swap flow includes a BIOS flash that reinitializes UEFI keys. We will capture flash logs for affected units.
2026-03-11 14:55 - Security (Maya Gupta): Enrolled new signing key in internal MOK test harness; need to align signing process and update runbook — advisories incoming.

2026-03-12 10:02 - Samir Patel: Temporary instructions created for depot techs to MOK-enroll after repair; preparing permanent fix to re-sign modules with vendor-trusted key and update image signing step.
source.resolution: Workaround: depot techs may enroll the new module signing key using mokutil and complete the MOK enrollment cycle at repair handoff. Permanent fix: imaging pipeline updated to sign corp-nic.ko with vendor-trusted signing certificate accepted by current Secure Boot MOK/DB; CI job created to verify module acceptance as part of image build. Follow-ups: 1) Add a preflight verification step in depot image build that boots with Secure Boot enforced and checks module load; 2) Update Depot Runbook to include MOK key rotation checklist; 3) Security to coordinate with vendor to get our signing certificate in vendor-signed catalog if feasible. Pending: rollout of updated image and validation across 50 sample units scheduled for 2026-03-18.

source.summary: Delayed Okta group propagation + identity-proxy session cache mismatch causing 403 on feature-flag console role swap
source.description: Users who are moved between Okta groups (role change) intermittently receive 403 when switching roles in the feature-flag console. The UI shows a successful role swap, but subsequent API calls return 403 until the user logs out or their session is refreshed. Issue appears tied to delayed group propagation from Okta and stale claim caching in identity-proxy.
source.impact: Several platform engineers and on-call responders cannot access feature-flag controls immediately after role changes, blocking emergency toggles and delaying incident remediation. Estimated ~8-12 users impacted in on-call rotation during the window of group propagation (observed in last 48 hours).
steps_to_reproduce: 1) In Okta, move a user from 'devtools_readonly' to 'devtools_admin' group
2) In the feature-flag console, click 'Switch role' to elevated role (console shows switch succeeded),
3) Attempt an admin action (toggle a flag) -> API returns 403 even though UI indicates admin role
4) Refresh browser or sign out/in -> action succeeds immediately.
logs: identity-proxy logs (2026-03-11T14:02:30Z): cached_claims_lookup user=akhumar ttl=600s returned roles=[devtools_readonly]
okta-system logs (2026-03-11T14:02:12Z): group_membership.patch user=akhumar groups=[devtools_admin] accepted, processing queued
console-api (2026-03-11T14:03:05Z): authz denied user=akhumar reason=missing_role:devtools_admin status=403 request_id=cf3a2d
investigation_notes: - Observed gap ~20-90s between Okta group change acceptance and SCIM/okta-event consumer propagation to identity service.
- Identity-proxy caches claims per-session for 10 minutes by default (cache TTL=600s). On role change we rely on event-driven invalidation but consumer misses short-lived events during burst.
- Reproduced in staging by toggling group membership and watching identity-proxy claim cache not invalidating until TTL expiry or explicit logout.
- Correlated with spike of Okta webhook retries due to transient 503s on event-consumer (see related_slack #idp-events thread).
- Temporary workaround validated: explicit POST /internal/v1/sessions/{sid}/refresh triggers reclaim and restores access immediately.
- Related dashboards: idp.events.consumer.error-rate, identity-proxy.cache.hit-rate, console.authz.
comments: Aisha Kumar (2026-03-10): Filed after on-call reported inability to toggle flags during incident. Provided user trace and quick repro.

Ben Carter (2026-03-11): Confirmed repro in staging. Identity-proxy cache TTL and webhook delivery delays are the probable vector. Will instrument additional logging and add a manual cache invalidation endpoint.
Diego Morales (SRE) (2026-03-11): Noted event-consumer had transient 503s from 13:58-14:05 UTC; retries might have caused ordering/dedup issues. Increasing retry backoff and adding jitter proposed.
Marketing Ops (2026-03-12): Impacted during a customer demo — need prioritization. Please provide ETA for mitigation.

Ben Carter (2026-03-12): ETA for hotfix to add synchronous claim-refresh on console role-switch: 48 hours. Patch PR opened: see related_github_prs. Long-term: event-driven invalidation reliability improvements and reduce claim-TTL to 60s for interactive sessions.
next_steps: 1) Ship identity-proxy PR to add immediate claim-refresh endpoint and wire console role-switch to call it (hotfix) - in progress
2) Patch idp-event-consumer to add backoff/jitter and improve delivery metrics - planned
3) Short-term config change: reduce interactive session claim TTL from 600s to 60s via feature flag for experiments
4) Add automated test that simulates Okta group-change -> event -> cache invalidation to prevent regression
5) Postmortem and runbook update describing manual session refresh endpoint and recommended operator steps.

source.summary: Provision ephemeral IAM broker role and cross-account access for runtime debugging across GCP/AWS
source.description:
Issue summary:
We need a short-lived, auditable broker role that lets SRE/Platform engineers assume cross-account access into customer-facing Dedicated and Private environments (AWS/GCP) for live debugging and incident response. The broker should centralize approval, rotate credentials automatically, and emit audit events to the central logging pipeline.

Background:
Recent incidents required manual cross-account key exchange and one-off service-account impersonation. That flow caused delays and missing audit context when engineers debugged GPU-backed inference stacks in customer staging and prod. We want a managed ephemeral path that enforces least privilege and automatic expiry.

Impact:
- Time-to-diagnose is increased for Sev1/Sev2 incidents affecting dedicated/private customers.
- Manual processes create orphaned access snapshots and inconsistent audit trails.

Request / Acceptance criteria:
- Provision a broker IAM role (AWS) and a service-account impersonation flow (GCP) that can be assumed via a short-lived session (max 1 hour).
- Central approval: integrate with internal approval workflow (Slack + Approvals API) and capture approver identity in audit logs.
- RBAC mapping: broker maps to scoped roles (e.g., readonly-serving, gpu-debug) rather than full admin.
- Automatic credential lifecycle: no long-lived keys; sessions are ephemeral and recorded.
- Logging: all assume/impersonation events logged to central Cloud Logging and forwarded to SIEM.
- Revocation: ability to immediately revoke active sessions and list active sessions per engineer.

Security constraints and notes:
- No direct customer secrets or keys are exposed in Slack.
- Use KMS/HSM-backed signing for temporary credentials issuance where supported.
- All changes must be reviewed by Security before prod enablement.

Expiry policy and audit retention:
- Default session TTL: 30 minutes; configurable up to 1 hour with justification.
- Audit records retained according to compliance policy (90 days hot, 1 year cold).

Owners and timeline:
- Owner: Diego Morales (Platform/SRE)
source.steps_to_reproduce: N/A (operational request). Example of current manual flow: 1) Engineer requests cross-account SA key via #priv-access, 2) Infra provides short-lived key in DM, 3) Engineer uses key to access customer VPC and run debug commands. This flow lacks centralized approval metadata and consistent logging.
comments: 2026-03-10 09:12 - Ava Chen: Logged initial request after postmortem for SUP-20242 highlighted delays in getting access to dedicated-prod us-east GPU nodes.
2026-03-10 14:03 - Diego Morales: Acked. Will draft a terraform module and a minimal approval Webhook. Need SRE & Security input on allowed role mappings.
2026-03-11 10:20 - Priya Nair (Security): Proposed constraints: deny iam:PassRole to the broker, restrict to a whitelist of target accounts, require MFA reauth for sessions longer than 15 minutes.
2026-03-12 08:45 - Diego Morales: Created branch infra/ephemeral-iam-broker and pushed initial TF module. Linked PR in related_github_prs. WIP: unit tests and integration with approvals service.
investigation_notes: Reviewed existing INT-11456 (scoped debug role) for patterns to reuse resource naming and policies.
Checked current org trust relationships for aws:role assumption and gcp:iam.serviceAccounts.actAs permissions; some accounts require org-level changes.
Evaluated open-source brokers (HashiCorp Boundary, short-lived session helpers) — decided to implement a lightweight internal broker to keep tighter audit hooks.
Identified required change windows for accounts with strict Service Perimeter (GCP) — will coordinate with Cloud Platform team for rollout.
Mapping proposal: broker -> scoped role policies: readonly-serving, gpu-debug, network-trace. Each mapping will be a separate policy attached at request time.
resolution_notes: WIP: terraform module created in branch infra/ephemeral-iam-broker; PR opened for review (see related_github_prs).

Next steps: 1) Security review (Priya), 2) Automation for Slack approval -> Approvals API webhook (Diego), 3) Integration test with staging accounts, 4) Runbook and oncall training, 5) Prod enablement behind feature-flag.
If approved, will schedule a small canary with SRE on 2026-03-24 to validate session expiry and revocation flows.

source.summary: SSO auth proxy header collision causes intermittent 401/403 for analytics gateway login
source.description:
Issue summary:
Users and SREs reported intermittent 401/403 when logging into the analytics portal and when machine agents call the insights API from app-hosted flows. Errors began appearing after proxy middleware v2.14.0 was deployed to prod on 2026-03-09.

Impact:
- ~8% of analytics-portal login attempts fail during peak minutes (observed 09:00–11:00 PDT).
- Automated ingestion jobs that rely on service-to-service SSO occasionally fail with 401s, triggering retries and delayed dashboards.

Environment:
- Prod (us-west-2) behind identity-proxy fleet (k8s, istio sidecar + custom auth middleware).

Observed behavior:
- Requests that should be allowed by Okta SSO show a 401 from the analytics-gateway.
- When retries succeed, request headers show slightly different casing/order for auth-related headers.
- No broad Okta outage; only services routed through the updated proxy are affected.

First seen: 2026-03-09 08:54 PDT (alerts from support + SRE dashboards).
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Hit https://analytics.redwood.com and start login via Okta SSO (browser redirect flow).
2) Observe the redirect back to analytics-portal with session cookie and Authorization header from identity-proxy.
3) Occasionally receive 401 from analytics-gateway after header verification (reproducible in ~1 in 10 attempts during spike).
4) For service-to-service: curl insights API from an internal host through proxy with valid service token; observe intermittent 401s in the service logs.
logs: 2026-03-10T09:15:12Z identity-proxy[proxy-xx] INFO - incoming request /login - headers: {Authorization: Bearer <redacted>, X-Forwarded-User: 'ethan.park', x-okta-session: 'abc123'}
2026-03-10T09:15:12Z analytics-gateway[gateway-yy] WARN - auth middleware: missing expected header 'X-User-Claim' -> returning 401
2026-03-10T09:15:12Z identity-proxy[proxy-xx] DEBUG - header-normalizer applied canonicalization: 'x-user-claim' overwritten by 'X-User-Claim' mapping
2026-03-10T09:16:40Z insights-api[ingest-zz] ERROR - auth check failed for token validation: upstream returned 401; headers seen: Authorization, x-user-claim
comments: 2026-03-10 09:32 - Support (Aisha): Noted spike in 401s on alerts channel #support. Collected a few failing request ids and sample headers from customers. Asked SRE to triage proxy changes rolled last night.

2026-03-10 10:01 - SRE (Samira): Confirmed recent deployment of proxy middleware v2.14.0 (https://github.com/redwood-inference/proxy/releases/tag/v2.14.0). Noticed new header-normalizer component that rewrites auth header keys to lowercase to fix legacy clients. Rolling back candidate sent to canary for testing.

2026-03-10 11:05 - Eng (Diego): Ran reproduce script from staging; observed header duplication where both 'x-user-claim' and 'X-User-Claim' are present; downstream analytics-gateway validates only 'X-User-Claim' exact case, causing intermittent mismatches when proxies compress/merge headers.

2026-03-11 08:24 - SRE (Samira): Deployed a hotfix to proxy configuration (disable header-normalizer for auth-related headers) to canary cluster. Early canary shows failure rate drop from 8% -> 0.5% for canary traffic.
2026-03-12 14:40 - Reporter (Ethan): Confirming login success rate looks normal for morning traffic. Will monitor for 48h before closing.
source.investigation_notes: Timeline and evidence: v2.14.0 introduced 'header-normalizer' that lowercases and deduplicates headers. Analytics-gateway auth middleware performs exact-case lookup for 'X-User-Claim' and sometimes reads the wrong header name when the proxy rewrites ordering/casing. Reproducible in staging when header-normalizer enabled. Root cause: tight coupling between proxy normalization and gateway's case-sensitive header expectation. No Okta token failures observed. Metrics: error budget burn tied to proxy deployment window.
source.hypothesis: Header canonicalization in proxy created a race/duplication where downstream service reads the wrong header key; some ingress paths merge headers differently causing gateway to miss the user claim and reject the request. The true fix is to either stop normalizing auth headers or make downstream header lookup case-insensitive/standardized.
source.mitigation: Temporarily disabled header-normalizer for auth-related headers in prod proxy config (canary -> full rollout). Added a gateway fallback to check both 'X-User-Claim' and 'x-user-claim' while the full fix is implemented. Rolled out within 3 hours of detection; immediate error-rate improvement observed.
source.resolution: Permanent fix implemented: updated proxy middleware to exclude auth and okta-related headers from normalization and added service-side tolerant header reading. Changes merged in PR https://github.com/redwood-inference/proxy/pull/842 and https://github.com/redwood-inference/analytics-portal/pull/191. Deployed to prod with canary stage, then 25% -> 100% rollout over 6 hours on 2026-03-12.
follow_up_actions: 1) Add unit/integration tests for header canonicalization behavior (proxy + gateway).
2) Update runbook entry to include header-normalizer exclusion list for auth headers.
3) Postmortem doc and timeline entry on Confluence (owner: Samira, due 2026-03-19).
4) Audit other services that may rely on exact-case header reads and apply tolerant lookups.
5) Add alert to detect sudden jump in header key variance for auth flows.

title: SSO auth proxy header collision causes intermittent 401/403 for analytics gateway login
preview:
Issue summary:
Users and SREs reported intermittent 401/403 when logging into the analytics portal and when machine agents call the insights API from app-hosted flows. Errors began appearing after proxy middleware v2.14.0 was deployed to prod on 2026-03-09.

Impact:
- ~8% of analytics-portal login attempts fail during peak minutes (observed 09:00–11:00 PDT).
- Automated ingestion jobs that rely on service-to-service SSO occasionally fail with 401s, triggering retries and delayed dashboards.

Environment:
- Prod (us-west-2) behind identity-proxy fleet (k8s, istio sidecar + custom auth middleware).

Observed behavior:
- Requests that should be allowed by Okta SSO show a 401 from the analytics-gateway.
- When retries succeed, request headers show slightly different casing/order for auth-related headers.
- No broad Okta outage; only services routed through the updated proxy are affected.

First seen: 2026-03-09 08:54 PDT (alerts from support + SRE dashboards).

source.summary: Intermittent high latency and packet loss for staff connected to corporate VPN over office Wi-Fi — suspected MTU/fragmentation
source.description: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours several engineers on the us-west office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA) reported intermittent high latency (200–800ms) and packet loss when accessing internal services hosted in our VPC while connected to the corporate IPSec VPN. Impact: Developer workflow degraded (git pushes, large artifact downloads time out), remote debugging sessions dropped. No impact reported for staff on wired desks or staff using the Dedicated VLAN SSID. Environment: Office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA SSID) on AireOS AP controller (firmware 8.10.140.0), IPSec gateway (vendor: FortiGate FG-300E) with UDP encapsulation (NAT-T), client VPN is our standard AnyConnect replacement (custom), client OSes affected: Windows 10/11 and some macOS 12 clients. Steps to reproduce: 1) Connect to Corp-STA Wi‑Fi in us-west office. 2) Establish VPN to corporate gateway. 3) Run: ping -s 1400 10.0.5.10 (internal service) or scp a ~50MB artifact from internal repo. 4) Observe latency spikes, occasional 'Destination Unreachable (Fragmentation needed)'. Observed behaviour: Large TCP transfers stall or timeout, ICMP 'fragmentation needed' seen originating from gateway/firewall in packet captures; traceroute shows stable path until AP controller/handoff point then hops spike. Expected behaviour: Stable sub-50ms latency with no packet loss over Wi‑Fi when connected to VPN. Attachments: packet_capture_corrupted.pcap (sensitive), traceroute_logs.txt, AP-controller-config-snippet.txt
steps_to_reproduce: Join Corp-STA SSID (us-west) with corporate credentials
Start VPN client and authenticate
From laptop run: ping -s 1400 10.0.5.10 -c 10
Attempt git clone or scp of a ~50MB repo/artifact
Observe intermittent timeouts and high round-trip times
logs_and_outputs: tcpdump snippet (client side): IP 192.168.10.42 > 10.0.5.10: frag needed and DF set, next MTU 1400
Gateway syslog: %FGD_SSL: pkgid 0: icmp type=3 code=4 src=10.0.5.10 dst=192.168.10.42 proto=1
Traceroute from client: 10.0.0.1 (AP-controller) -> 10.10.50.1 (core-switch) shows jump to 250-700ms at hop 5
Windows network stack: MSS observed 1460 but post-encapsulation packet size exceeds path MTU
AP controller config: wireless mtu 1600 (non-default) for performance tuning
investigation_notes: 10MAR: Support verified reproducible packet loss with large ICMP payloads when VPN is active on Wi‑Fi clients; wired clients unaffected
10MAR: tcpdump on client shows ICMP type 3 code 4 (fragmentation needed) matching FTG gateway messages
10MAR: PMTU discovery appears to be failing for ESP/UDP encapsulated packets — the DF bit blocks fragmentation and some middleboxes do not forward ICMP 'fragmentation needed' back to client
11MAR: Temporary test: lowered client MTU to 1400 -> transfers succeed consistently; indicates MTU is root cause not wireless signal
11MAR: AP controller configured to advertise 1600 MTU on some radio profiles for aggregation; that combined with IPsec overhead produces effective packet sizes > path MTU
11MAR: FortiGate currently not performing MSS clamping for TCP-over-UDP-encapsulated VPN traffic; vendor ticket opened but patch ETA unknown
11MAR: Observed intermittent higher CPU on a subset of APs coinciding with degraded sessions — may exacerbate retransmits but not primary cause
comments: 2026-03-10 10:45 - Ava Chen (Reporter): Team noticed repeated failures with 'Destination Unreachable - fragmentation needed' while trying to push code to internal git over VPN. Wired desks are fine. Filing ticket for network team.
2026-03-10 11:30 - Diego Martinez (Assignee): Acknowledged. Captures requested; please attach pcap/trace. I'll spin up a test client in lab and reproduce.

2026-03-10 14:05 - SRE (Priya Nair): Able to reproduce from a test laptop. PMTU ICMPs are emitted by gateway but not always reaching clients. Workaround: reduce MTU to 1400 on client or connect to Dedicated-VLAN SSID.
2026-03-11 09:12 - Networking (Marcus Hale): AP controller shows non-standard MTU on radio profile for aggregation. We will roll back that profile to default 1500 on a subset of APs for test.
2026-03-11 11:00 - Diego Martinez: Rolled back radio profile on AP floor 3/4. Users report improvements; coordinating with FortiGate team for MSS clamp rule as permanent fix.
2026-03-12 08:23 - Marcus Hale: Opened change request to add MSS clamping rule on firewall for UDP-encapsulated VPN (CR-2026-0312). Scheduled for 2026-03-16 maintenance window.
2026-03-12 12:40 - Diego Martinez: Recommend communication to office staff with temporary workaround (use wired where possible or lower MTU to 1400).
action_items: Network: Implement MSS clamping rule on FortiGate for VPN UDP-encapsulated traffic (owner: Marcus Hale) - target 2026-03-16
Wireless: Revert custom MTU profile on remaining APs to 1500 and monitor client error rates (owner: Marcus Hale) - target 2026-03-13
Support: Communicate temporary workaround to office via #internal-it and signage (owner: Ava Chen) - ASAP
Engineering: Add automated health check for PMTU failures in Redwood Console internal dashboards (owner: SRE team) - follow-up ticket
resolution: Temporary mitigation: lower client MTU to 1400 or connect to Dedicated-VLAN SSID; rollback of non-default AP MTU profile completed on floors 3/4 which reduced incidence.
Planned permanent fix: Add MSS clamping on FortiGate for UDP-encapsulated VPN and ensure AP profiles advertise standard MTU; change scheduled for 2026-03-16 maintenance window.
Post-incident: SRE to add a PMTU alert rule and update runbook with 'MTU mismatch' checklist and communication templates.

source.summary: Post-SSD repair: systemd-resume hang after hibernation prevents fleet enrollment and SCCM check-in on Ubuntu 22.04
source.description:
Issue summary:
After SSD replacement and standard depot provisioning, several Ubuntu 22.04 laptops are failing to complete post-repair check-in. The devices appear to boot normally but never reach fleet enrollment (fleet-agent) and SCCM client check-in. Investigations show the machine is stuck in the systemd 'resume' target after a hibernation/resume cycle triggered by the depot hardware test.

Impact:
- Affected devices are not joining inventory or receiving policy, delaying redeployment to users.
- ~6 devices reported from depot on 2026-03-08 and 2026-03-09.
- Operational impact: rework and manual reprovisioning required for redeployments.

Environment:
- Ubuntu 22.04 LTS (custom corporate image built via imaging pipeline)
- kernel 5.15.0-80-generic (corp-pinned)
- Fleet-agent v1.8.2, SCCM agent shim for Linux v2.1.0
- SSD vendor: Western Digital SN730 (replacement units from depot pool)
- Boot mode: UEFI, Secure Boot enabled

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Depot engineer replaces NVMe SSD with stock SN730 unit and re-flashes corporate image via imaging station (PXE → task sequence).
2) Imaging completes and system runs hardware self-test which forces a suspend/hibernate cycle at end of task sequence (this is new in depot script rev 2026-02-28).
3) Machine resumes and reaches systemd resume.target but several services never transition to running (fleet-agent, sccm-shim).
4) Device appears responsive over serial/console, but fleet enroll never completes and SCCM check-in does not occur.

Observed logs / artifacts:
- journalctl -b shows repeated timeouts in systemd-resume.service and a backtrace for kernel resume_resume_device for NVMe (attached lines below).
- /var/log/fleet-agent.log ends with "waiting for network" but networking shows UP from ip link.
- dmesg shows "nvme 0000:01:00.0: failed to resume queue 0" intermittently.

Investigation notes:
- Hypothesis 1: NVMe driver resume path regression when using replacement SN730 units + current kernel interacting with corporate init ordering.
- Hypothesis 2: the depot's testing script forces a hibernate state that trips a systemd ordering edge case where resume.target occurs before network.target, causing fleet-agent's registration to wait indefinitely behind a masked wait.
- Reproduced on one lab device by explicitly triggering hibernate after imaging; same symptoms observed.
- Booting with "noresume" kernel param allows normal boot and fleet enrollment proceeds, indicating resume code path is implicated.
- Kernel upgrade test candidate (5.15.0-90) in staging reduced resume hang frequency but did not fully eliminate.
- Vendor NVMe firmware on affected replacement units is 1.0.3; depot stock contains mixed firmware versions.

Temporary workaround:
- Depot engineers should avoid triggering the hibernate step in the post-imaging script (reverted to previous script version 2026-02-21) until a fix is validated.
- For devices already affected: boot with kernel parameter "noresume" then re-trigger normal boot to allow fleet enrollment; technicians can then re-image if desired.

Next steps / action items:
- Imaging team to roll back depot script to pre-hibernate version (owner: Priya Shah, due 2026-03-12).
- Kernel team to validate whether kernel resume path fixes in 5.15.0-90/5.19 backports resolve the NVMe resume failures (owner: Ethan Park, in progress).
- Procurement/dev-ops to audit depot NVMe firmware and segregate SN730 units with firmware 1.0.3 (owner: Marco Li, due 2026-03-15).
- Create focused test matrix: SSD vendor/firmware x kernel version x hibernate enabled/disabled. Capture reproducibility matrix and logs (owner: Imaging QA).

Acceptance criteria for resolution:
- Depot imaging script can run with hibernate test and devices consistently resume and complete fleet enrollment without manual intervention.
- Root cause documented and either kernel patch, firmware update, or imaging change implemented.
comments: 2026-03-10 09:14 - Priya Shah: Noticed three units from Depot B failing check-in this morning. Attached serial logs and the imaging task sequence used.
2026-03-10 11:02 - Ethan Park: Reproduced on a local test bench by triggering hibernate after imaging. Kernel noresume avoids the issue — pointing to resume path. Will try 5.15.0-90 in staging.
2026-03-11 08:30 - Marco Li: Procurement report: sample of depot SN730s show mixed firmware. Segregating units with 1.0.3 and flagging for firmware update.
2026-03-12 14:45 - Priya Shah: Imaging pipeline rolled back to script v2026-02-21 in Depots A/B. Depot engineers instructed to skip hibernate step until further notice.
2026-03-12 16:10 - Ethan Park: Kernel backport test shows reduced frequency; still seeing 1/10 repros. Next: coordinate with NVMe vendor on firmware behavior and gather specific device serials.
2026-03-12 16:45 - Dana Morales: Added affected serial numbers to ticket and flagged for physical QC check during next depot batch.
