source.summary: NetworkManager writes duplicate default routes during imaging causing AAD join timeout in ap-southeast
source.description:
Issue summary:
During automated task-sequence imaging in APAC (ap-southeast-1), NetworkManager on fresh Windows PE handoff writes two overlapping default routes (0.0.0.0/0) to the primary interface. The duplicated routes have different metrics and intermittently cause the Azure AD join step to time out (AADSTS network error) or attempt to use an internal management VPN endpoint, failing domain registration and stalling the task sequence.

Impact:
- ~5-8% of devices in the last 48h fail AAD join during imaging and are left in the 'Autopilot not completed' state.
- Affected devices require manual intervention (local admin join + re-run of post-provision scripts) delaying depot turn-around.
- Mostly impacts APAC staging fleet and a small subset of production depot images.

Environment:
- Imaging orchestrator: SCCM task sequence handing off to WinPE -> OOBE scripts (internal imaging-playbooks v3.7).
- Network manager: Custom NM shim used in WinPE handoff (win-nm-wrapper v0.9.2).
- Observed on Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen11, Dell Latitude 7440.
- Regions: ap-southeast-1 staging and select prod depots.

Steps to reproduce (intermittent):
1) Boot device into SCCM task sequence using depot network VLAN (APAC depot).
2) WinPE hands off to windows setup, imaging-playbook triggers win-nm-wrapper to apply DHCP/route config.
3) Watch netstat / route print or use PowerShell Get-NetRoute after handoff.
4) Observe two default routes: one with metric 15 and one with metric 5, sometimes bound to same interface.
5) Continue imaging -> Azure AD Join step times out or reports network unreachable.

Observed behavior:
- Duplicate default routes appear immediately after win-nm-wrapper runs; sometimes the lower-metric route points at a management VLAN gateway that should be unreachable from the depot VLAN.
- AAD join logs show network requests routed to the wrong gateway and either timing out or returning HTTP 502 from an internal proxy.
- Re-running networking script that deletes the duplicate route allows AAD join to succeed.

Expected behavior:
- Single default route to depot gateway (correct metric).
- AAD join uses depot network path to Microsoft endpoints.

Logs / Artifacts attached in ticket: list of sample Get-NetRoute outputs, win-nm-wrapper logs, SCCM task sequence trace snippets, sample AAD join traces (correlation IDs) and pcap from one repro.

Investigation notes:
- Initial hypothesis: race between DHCP client registering routes and win-nm-wrapper adding explicit route entries leads to duplicate entries.
- win-nm-wrapper v0.9.2 introduced a change to add a default-route fallback with lower metric when DHCP lease is delayed (PR infra/imaging-playbooks#157). Suspect this path improperly fires even when DHCP later populates the route.
- Correlated failures with depot switches that have intermittent DHCP lease latency (~100-400ms) during peak provisioning windows.
- Reproduced locally by delaying DHCP response in a lab: when wrapper adds fallback route and DHCP later adds its route, system ends up with two default routes. Windows route selection uses metric so traffic can go to unintended gateway.

Temporary workaround:
- Add a cleanup step in task sequence immediately after win-nm-wrapper: run PowerShell to remove duplicate default routes, keeping the DHCP-provided one (script snippet available). Deployed to staging depots, reduced failures to ~0.5% in first 12h.

Next steps / action items:
- Engineering to patch win-nm-wrapper to check for an existing default route and avoid adding fallback when DHCP is expected (owner: infra/imaging team).
- Long-term: update imaging-playbooks to wait for DHCP lease or implement an idempotent route reconcile step; add unit test for delayed DHCP scenarios.
- Ops: monitor APAC depot provisioning metrics and roll the cleanup script to remaining depots pending patch.

Resolution (work in progress):
- Temporary cleanup script deployed to 2 staging depots -- effective as a stopgap.
- Patch PR opened: infra/imaging-playbooks#157 (fix adds route-existence check and 2s wait for DHCP). Pending code review and canary.
comments: 2026-03-10 09:42 - Ethan Park: Opened ticket after 6 failures observed in APAC staging depot between 03/08-03/09. Collected route prints and AAD join logs; attached artifacts.
2026-03-10 11:05 - Meera Patel: Confirmed repro in lab by delaying DHCP. Created temp PS cleanup and deployed to staging node group. Requesting ENG patch against win-nm-wrapper.

2026-03-11 08:20 - SRE (Diego Morales): Noted DHCP server logs show occasional lease latency spikes correlated with provisioning peaks. Coordinating with network team to validate switch load and DHCP performance.
2026-03-11 14:33 - Infra Eng (Nina S.): Patch PR opened (infra/imaging-playbooks#157) to make route add idempotent and wait for DHCP up to 3s; added unit test. Marked for canary in staging depots.
2026-03-12 09:12 - Meera Patel: Canary started on 3 devices in staging; initial runs show no duplicate route after wrapper. Monitoring for 48h before prod rollout.
2026-03-12 16:40 - Ethan Park: Ops has scheduled cleanup script rollout to remaining staging depots tonight. Please keep ticket updated with canary results and prod rollout plan.

title: NetworkManager writes duplicate default routes during imaging causing AAD join timeout in ap-southeast
preview:
Issue summary:
During automated task-sequence imaging in APAC (ap-southeast-1), NetworkManager on fresh Windows PE handoff writes two overlapping default routes (0.0.0.0/0) to the primary interface. The duplicated routes have different metrics and intermittently cause the Azure AD join step to time out (AADSTS network error) or attempt to use an internal management VPN endpoint, failing domain registration and stalling the task sequence.

Impact:
- ~5-8% of devices in the last 48h fail AAD join during imaging and are left in the 'Autopilot not completed' state.
- Affected devices require manual intervention (local admin join + re-run of post-provision scripts) delaying depot turn-around.
- Mostly impacts APAC staging fleet and a small subset of production depot images.

Environment:
- Imaging orchestrator: SCCM task sequence handing off to WinPE -> OOBE scripts (internal imaging-playbooks v3.7).
- Network manager: Custom NM shim used in WinPE handoff (win-nm-wrapper v0.9.2).
- Observed on Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen11, Dell Latitude 7440.
- Regions: ap-southeast-1 staging and select prod depots.

Steps to reproduce (intermittent):
1) Boot device into SCCM task sequence using depot network VLAN (APAC depot).
2) WinPE hands off to windows setup, imaging-playbook triggers win-nm-wrapper to apply DHCP/route config.
3) Watch netstat / route print or use PowerShell Get-NetRoute after handoff.
4) Observe two default routes: one with metric 15 and one with metric 5, sometimes bound to same interface.
5) Continue imaging -> Azure AD Join step times out or reports network unreachable.

Observed behavior:
- Duplicate default routes appear immediately after win-nm-wrapper runs; sometimes the lower-metric route points at a management VLAN gateway that should be unreachable from the depot VLAN.
- AAD join logs show network requests routed to the wrong gateway and either timing out or returning HTTP 502 from an internal proxy.
- Re-running networking script that deletes the duplicate route allows AAD join to succeed.

Expected behavior:
- Single default route to depot gateway (correct metric).
- AAD join uses depot network path to Microsoft endpoints.

Logs / Artifacts attached in ticket: list of sample Get-NetRoute outputs, win-nm-wrapper logs, SCCM task sequence trace snippets, sample AAD join traces (correlation IDs) and pcap from one repro.

Investigation notes:
- Initial hypothesis: race between DHCP client registering routes and win-nm-wrapper adding explicit route entries leads to duplicate entries.
- win-nm-wrapper v0.9.2 introduced a change to add a default-route fallback with lower metric when DHCP lease is delayed (PR infra/imaging-playbooks#157). Suspect this path improperly fires even when DHCP later populates the route.
- Correlated failures with depot switches that have intermittent DHCP lease latency (~100-400ms) during peak provisioning windows.
- Reproduced locally by delaying DHCP response in a lab: when wrapper adds fallback route and DHCP later adds its route, system ends up with two default routes. Windows route selection uses metric so traffic can go to unintended gateway.

Temporary workaround:
- Add a cleanup step in task sequence immediately after win-nm-wrapper: run PowerShell to remove duplicate default routes, keeping the DHCP-provided one (script snippet available). Deployed to staging depots, reduced failures to ~0.5% in first 12h.

Next steps / action items:
- Engineering to patch win-nm-wrapper to check for an existing default route and avoid adding fallback when DHCP is expected (owner: infra/imaging team).
- Long-term: update imaging-playbooks to wait for DHCP lease or implement an idempotent route reconcile step; add unit test for delayed DHCP scenarios.
- Ops: monitor APAC depot provisioning metrics and roll the cleanup script to remaining depots pending patch.

Resolution (work in progress):
- Temporary cleanup script deployed to 2 staging depots -- effective as a stopgap.
- Patch PR opened: infra/imaging-playbooks#157 (fix adds route-existence check and 2s wait for DHCP). Pending code review and canary.

source.summary: Authorization drift: tag-based IAM condition prevents dedicated-provisioner from completing cross-account role exchange in prod us-east
source.description:
Issue summary:
During an automated Dedicated capacity scale-up (provisioner job) in prod/us-east, the dedicated-provisioner is failing to complete the AWS->GCP cross-account role exchange step. New worker nodes are left in a provisioning loop and cannot register with the scheduling service.

Impact:
- Provisioner failures are blocking dedicated pool scaling for two customer-facing pools (internal monitoring shows ~22% reduced capacity).
- Intermittent customer API throttling for dedicated customers relying on autoscale; no data loss but degraded throughput.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated provisioning pipeline (prod:us-east)
- Affected components: dedicated-provisioner (k8s Job), STS assume-role workflow, GCP service account impersonation to read KMS and secret manager artifacts.

Steps to reproduce (observed in prod):
1) Trigger scale-up of dedicated pool via the autoscaler.
2) Provisioner creates AWS EC2 instances and invokes STS:AssumeRole with session tags set by scheduler.
3) AssumeRole returns success but downstream IAM condition rejects subsequent GCP impersonation (role-binding audit shows DENY at policy-eval for principal tag mismatch).

Observed behavior:
- Logs: "AccessDenied: IAM condition 'request.tag:team' mismatch" then "gcp-impersonation: principal denied".
- Provisioner retries until job backoff limit and then marks node as failed.

Expected behavior:
- AssumeRole + session tags should be honored and the downstream GCP identity broker should accept the mapped principal; provisioning should complete normally.

Attachments:
- Provisioner logs (prod-us-east, last 24h) attached to ticket.
- IAM policy snapshots (AWS role trust policy and GCP IAM binding) uploaded to infra artifacts.
comments: 2026-03-10 09:12 - Priya Singh (SRE): Noted repeated 'request.tag:team' condition failures in the AWS CloudTrail trace correlated with the failed provisioner runs. I pushed a temporary increase to scheduler backoff to reduce noise.

2026-03-10 11:03 - Diego Morales (Infra): Confirmed that assume-role responses include session tags, but the tag value 'team:infra-prod' does not match the expected tag 'team:infra' in the GCP IAM condition expression. Hypothesis: recent tagging normalization changed suffixing behavior in the scheduler.
investigation_notes: Checked AWS STS logs: sessions are issued with tags 'team=infra-prod' and 'env=prod'. Previously tags were 'team=infra'.
Reviewed GCP IAM Binding on service-project: binding contains condition requiring request.auth.claims['team'] == 'infra' (string equality), which fails with 'infra-prod'.

Terraform drift: tag normalization change introduced in infra module refactor (merged as infra#3876) added '-prod' suffix for certain pipeline-created principals. Related PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/3876
Replayed a local assume-role with and without suffix; reproduced GCP deny when suffix present.
resolution: Hotfix applied (2026-03-11 02:14) by Diego Morales: rolled back tag-normalization in the dedicated scheduler (reverted infra PR 3876) to restore previous 'team' tag values. This restored successful GCP IAM condition evaluation and provisioner resumed normal operations.

Post-incident remediation: created follow-up task INT-672002 to add tolerant IAM condition expressions that accept both 'infra' and 'infra-prod' values OR map tags via a short canonicalization step in the identity broker.
Long-term: owners (Infra + Auth) to coordinate a single canonical tagging contract and add automated unit tests that validate tag mappings end-to-end across STS->identity-broker->GCP.
Customer impact: No customer data exposed. Capacity returned to normal within ~45 minutes after hotfix; communicated to internal stakeholders and oncall.

source.summary: Delegated access approval hierarchy blocking console elevation for engineers after Okta group merge
source.description:
Issue summary:
A delegated access request for console elevation is stuck in the approval workflow for multiple engineers after an Okta group merge. The approval UI shows 'Pending - waiting for approver' even though required approvers are listed and have approved. This prevents engineers from obtaining temporary elevated roles to access production dashboards and runbooks.

Impact:
- 6 engineers are blocked from elevating to a support role across multiple teams (SRE, Platform).
- On-call rotation had to manually escalate via Slack and temporary token sharing, increasing operational risk.
- No customer-facing outages, but internal response time for incidents increased.

Environment:
- Production (us-east)
- Affected service: approval-microservice v1.3.2, rbac-service v2.0.1, okta-scim-sync job (daily)

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create a delegated access request via Console -> Access -> Request Elevation (select 'support-engineer' role).
2) Add approvers from two different teams (team leads who are members of Okta groups).
3) Confirm approvals in the Console UI. Approver shows as confirmed but request remains Pending.

Observed behavior:
- UI shows approver checkboxes checked and timestamps on approver actions, but the request state is still 'Pending' and no role assignment is created in RBAC service.
- approval-microservice logs contain repeated errors of parent-role lookup failures: "parent role id lookup returned null for role_id=role-1243"
- Okta SCIM sync shows a recent group merge event at 2026-03-09 23:05 UTC (group A merged into group B).

Expected behavior:
- After required approvers approve, the request transitions to 'Approved' and rbac-service issues the temporary role assignment with TTL for the requester.

Relevant logs and links:
- approval-microservice error sample: 
  2026-03-11T02:12:45Z ERROR approval: failed to compute approval cascade: null parent for role=role-1243 request_id=REQ-20260311-132
- Okta SCIM sync job trace: https://ci.redwood-inference.internal/okta-sync/5678
- Slack thread: https://slack.redwood.com/archives/C0ABCDEF1/p1678550400000500
- Related infra PR (applied partially): https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/4321

Temporary workaround:
- Support engineers with admin privileges performed a manual role assignment in rbac-service for affected users. This restores access but bypasses the normal approval audit trail.

Next steps planned:
1) Investigate why parent-role lookup returned null after Okta group merge. Hypothesis: role inheritance cache references an Okta group ID that was deleted during the merge and the approval-microservice does not handle a missing parent gracefully.
2) Re-run a scoped role-propagation job to rebuild role lineage cache for the merged groups.
3) Patch approval-microservice to treat missing parent references as soft-missing and surface a clearer error + auto-retry path.
4) Add monitoring/alerting on approval-failure rates and parent-lookup errors.
comments: 2026-03-10 15:42 - Ava Chen: Filed after being paged by SRE oncall. Reported multiple engineers blocked; included initial logs and slack thread.
2026-03-10 16:05 - Support Triage (Rohan Singh): Acknowledged; escalated to Identity team for initial triage. Added label 'okta'.

2026-03-11 02:30 - Identity Eng (Maya Ortega): Found repeated parent-role lookup nulls in approval-microservice. Correlated with Okta group merge that happened 2026-03-09 23:05 UTC. Investigating SCIM events and DB referential integrity for role mappings.

2026-03-11 08:17 - Platform Eng (Liam Patel): Ran ad-hoc role-propagation job for affected namespace (support-engineer). Confirmed the cache rebuild succeeded; two requests transitioned to 'Approved' automatically. Applied interim rollback to re-enable manual fallback path.
2026-03-12 10:02 - Security (Nina Roberts): Raised concern about manual role assignments bypassing audit trail. Requested a follow-up to capture approval metadata for manually-assigned cases.

2026-03-12 14:30 - Liam Patel: Preparing patch to approval-microservice (see PR https://github.com/redwood-inference/approval-microservice/pull/102). Plan: handle null parent gracefully, emit metric approval.parent_lookup_null, and auto-queue a one-off re-evaluation job.
investigation_notes: 2026-03-11: Hypothesis A - Okta group merge removed a group id referenced by role parent_id in RBAC DB. approval-microservice code assumes parent exists and throws, which prevents marking completed approvals.
2026-03-11: Hypothesis B - SCIM sync applied deletions in the wrong order during merge leading to transient dangling references (observed SCIM events: 'group.delete' followed by 'group.update').

2026-03-11: DB check - rbac.role_hierarchy shows role-1243.parent_id referencing okta-group-983 which is absent from okta_group mapping table. Evidence: SELECT * FROM okta_group WHERE id='okta-group-983' returned 0 rows.
2026-03-11: Reproduction - Replaying the merge event on staging with a copy of org metadata reproduced the parent lookup error. Approval microservice log trace identical.
source.resolution: In progress: Scoped role-propagation rebuild executed for the support role namespace which auto-cleared the majority of stuck requests. A code patch to approval-microservice has been created to handle missing parent references without failing the entire evaluation path (PR https://github.com/redwood-inference/approval-microservice/pull/102). Follow-ups: add metric approval.parent_lookup_null, alert at threshold, and add an automated recovery job that re-evaluates stuck requests daily. Planning a postmortem after full rollout to cover changes to SCIM merge ordering and add a playbook for manual recovery.
resolution_notes: 2026-03-11: Rolled a temporary role-propagation job (job id rp-20260311-01) for affected namespace; transitioned 4/6 stuck requests to Approved.
2026-03-12: Deployed approval-microservice patch to prod canary (10% traffic) — no new parent_lookup_null errors observed in 24h window on canary nodes.
Follow-up action: Identity to add a pre-merge validation step in okta-scim-sync to ensure role mappings have graceful fallback values. Timeline: 2 weeks.

source.summary: Clarify provisional license reclaim window and equipment-return grace period for immediate offboarding
source.description:
Issue summary:
HR received reports from Finance and IT that when an employee is placed on Immediate Termination (ITerm) in Workday, the seat/license in third-party SaaS (notably the design/legal tool vendor) remains billable for up to 14 days and the assigned corporate laptop sometimes remains tagged as 'active' in our asset management inventory. This ticket seeks clarification of the intended policy (grace windows) and the operational mechanics (which systems enforce the reclaim/return windows).

Impact:
- Potential short-term double-billing for SaaS seats when seats are quickly re-assigned.
- Delays in device recovery leading to inventory inaccuracies and security exposure for high-risk departures.
- Confusion between HR, IT, and Finance on expected timelines and required manual steps.

Environment:
- Identity sync: Okta -> SCIM -> downstream SaaS (prod)
- Asset tracking: FleetOps (prod) with inventory sync to ITSM
- HR system: Workday (prod)

Observed behavior / Steps to reproduce:
1) Create an employee termination in Workday with 'Immediate' flag at 09:00 UTC.
2) Workday emits termination event, HR webhook updates Okta.
3) Okta SCIM provisioning sends deactivation to SaaS vendor; we observe the user status change but vendor maintains seat as billable for a 14-day grace window (vendor policy).
4) FleetOps does not mark the laptop as 'awaiting pickup' until an asset-handback task is created manually by IT; automatic tagging is not triggered by the termination event.
5) Finance reports a new invoice line for an additional seat 3 days later after license reassign because the old seat was not reclaimed yet.

Logs / evidence: 
- Okta SCIM audit (okta-audit-2026-03-09.log) shows deactivation event at 09:02 UTC and response 200 from vendor with response body: {"status":"deprovision-scheduled","grace_days":14}.
- FleetOps event stream shows no asset lifecycle change tied to the Workday termination event (fleetops-events-2026-03-09.ndjson).
- Finance ticket FIN-1189 referencing unexpected seat charge for week of 2026-03-02.

Question / Requested clarification:
- What are the company-wide defaults for SaaS license reclaim windows and how many days are considered 'standard' vs 'exception' requiring manual immediate reclaim?
- For Immediate Terminations, do we have an established exception/accelerated flow that forces vendor-side seat revocation and device pickup tagging?
- Which teams are expected to take manual actions vs which actions should be automated by identity/asset tooling?

Desired outcome:
- A concise policy statement HR/IT/Finance can rely on for immediate-term offboards, including: default reclaim window, criteria for accelerating reclaim, and the single authoritative runbook link for immediate offboards.
comments: 2026-03-10 11:10 — Marco Ruiz (IT): Noted. In our logs the SCIM response includes vendor-side grace window. We don't currently override vendor windows. IT can manually request offboarding escalation with vendor support but that requires a ticket and often 24–48h processing.
2026-03-10 12:02 — Priya Shah (HR): Can we make 'Immediate' terminations trigger a one-click 'accelerate reclaim' in the HR portal? That would reduce churn between teams.

2026-03-11 09:47 — Ethan Park (Identity): Hypothesis: Okta sets user to 'suspended' and vendor interprets that as soft-deactivate and applies a vendor-side grace window. We can attempt to send a 'hard-delete' SCIM call for critical departures but need legal approval and vendor API support confirmation.

2026-03-11 13:15 — Jana Müller (Finance): Current invoices show an average of 2.3 days of overlap per reassign. Shortening reclaim window will materially affect monthly spend forecasts. Please include Finance in policy decisions.
2026-03-12 08:03 — Support Bot (automated): Linked CONFLUENCE: License Reclaim Policy draft updated (v0.4) — https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/HR/pages/License+Reclaim+Policy
investigation_notes: Okta->SCIM behavior: The connector implementation uses PATCH to set 'active' to false. Vendor accepts this and sets an internal grace policy (14 days) rather than immediate seat relinquish.

FleetOps integration: Asset lifecycle changes are only triggered by ITSM ticket creation at present; there is no webhook or SCIM-driven asset tagging for device pickup. This is a gap for immediate offboards.

Workday mapping: 'Immediate' termination is mapped to Okta attribute 'terminationType=immediate' but that attribute is not propagated to downstream SCIM calls currently; therefore downstream vendors cannot differentiate Immediate vs standard terminations.

Hypothesis: If Okta emitted a 'terminationType' custom attribute or we used a 'hard-delete' SCIM call on certain vendors, seats could be reclaimed faster, but vendor policies and contracts may prohibit forced immediate deletion (data retention concerns).
source.resolution_notes:
Tentative operational plan agreed on 2026-03-12:
1) Policy clarification: Default reclaim window remains vendor-determined; for 'Immediate' terminations, HR + IT will follow an accelerated flow.
2) Automation tasks: Identity team to add terminationType flag propagation to SCIM (Ethan Park) and to implement a 'hard-delete' option gated behind a documented approval step.
3) Asset tagging: FleetOps to listen for Workday/Okta terminationType events and auto-tag devices as 'awaiting-security-pickup' (IT will still approve pickup).
4) Finance: will be looped into the vendor contract review to identify vendors that support immediate seat removal vs those with mandatory grace windows.
5) Runbook: Create a concise 'Immediate Offboard' runbook in Confluence with decision tree and contact channel (#it-offboarding).
Follow-ups (action items): [INT-821408] implement SCIM terminationType propagation; [INT-821409] FleetOps webhook for immediate offboard tagging; legal to review vendor TOS for forced seat deletion.
Notes: Until automation is in place, manual vendor escalation is the workaround for high-risk departures. This ticket should remain open until the SCIM flag propagation PR (INT-821408) is merged and FleetOps webhook (INT-821409) is live.

title: Clarify provisional license reclaim window and equipment-return grace period for immediate offboarding

This ticket seeks clarification of the intended policy (grace windows) and the operational mechanics (which systems enforce the reclaim/return windows).

Impact:
- Potential short-term double-billing for SaaS seats when seats are quickly re-assigned.
- Delays in device recovery leading to inventory inaccuracies and security exposure for high-risk departures.

- Okta SCIM audit (okta-audit-2026-03-09.log) shows deactivation event at 09:02 UTC and response 200 from vendor with response body: {"status":"deprovision-scheduled","grace_days":14}.
- FleetOps event stream shows no asset lifecycle change tied to the Workday termination event (fleetops-events-2026-03-09.ndjson).

source.summary: Access grant marked approved but onboarding sync never created runbooks-v2 role bindings
source.description: Issue summary: A recent internal access request for the 'runbooks-v2' tool is showing as APPROVED in the approval queue, but the provisioning pipeline did not create the corresponding RBAC role bindings or service tokens. Impact: impacted users cannot access runbooks-v2 dashboards or runbook actions; several oncall requests are blocked. This appears to affect only the new onboarding flow that uses the lifecycle-sync lambda rather than the legacy backfill.
source.impact: 3 engineers (including oncall) blocked from runbooks-v2; workflow escalations piling up; potential oncall delays
steps_to_reproduce: 1) Submit access request for runbooks-v2 via internal access UI
2) Approve request through manager approval step (approver receives notification and clicks approve),
3) Observe approval status turns to APPROVED in queue,
4) Verify that no role binding appears in rbac-service and no service token is issued in vault-provisioner
5) Attempt to access runbooks-v2 -> receive 403 / entitlement missing
logs: approval-queue: request_id=ac-20260310-77 status=APPROVED timestamp=2026-03-10T14:12:08Z
lifecycle-sync lambda: invocation for request_id=ac-20260310-77 ended with exit code 0 but no operations logged to rbac-service (logs show early return path)
rbac-service: no binding created for subject user: ava.chen@redwood.ai within window 2026-03-10T14:12Z - 2026-03-10T14:20Z
vault-provisioner: no token mint recorded for request_id=ac-20260310-77
investigation_notes: Initial hypothesis: lifecycle-sync executed but hit a guard that prevented creating bindings for identities without 'account_namespace' claim. The new access UI omits account_namespace for cross-org roles.
Reproduced locally by submitting a request with blank account_namespace; lifecycle-sync returns early from precondition check (code path added in patch rprovisioner #441).
Guard introduced in PR https://github.com/redwood-inference/provisioner/pull/441 to avoid creating bindings for deprecated role types; rollback candidate.
Observed that legacy backfill-based onboarding still creates bindings because it sets account_namespace explicitly. This explains partial impact.
SRE note: there is no obvious 5xx or timeout in lifecycle-sync — the function appears healthy and reports success; logic prevented work.
resolution_notes: Workaround (applied 2026-03-11 09:40Z): Manually backfilled RBAC bindings for affected users via rbac-service admin endpoint and recorded in audit log.

Short-term fix (in progress): revert the precondition check added in PR 441 and deploy a patch that treats missing account_namespace as 'default' for runbooks-v2 onboarding. Patch branch: fix/onboarding-fallback-882.

Long-term follow-up: update access UI to include account_namespace field for all onboarding flows and add unit tests covering this precondition. Create a ticket to add an automated reconciliation job to surface approved-but-not-provisioned requests.
Postmortem owner: Raj Patel. Target postmortem completion: 2026-03-20.
comments: Ava Chen (Reporter) [2026-03-10T15:02Z]: Users reported 403 when clicking into runbooks-v2 after approval. I can share a request id privately.
Raj Patel (Assignee) [2026-03-10T16:10Z]: Investigating — I see the lifecycle-sync runs but returns without calling rbac-service. Pulling recent provisioner PRs now.
SRE Team (2026-03-10T18:24Z): Confirmed lifecycle-sync logs show early-return path triggered by missing account_namespace claim. Recommending manual backfill for oncall users.
Dev (Kim Ortega) [2026-03-11T08:02Z]: Found PR 441 added the guard. Preparing a small rollback to remove the guard for runbooks-v2 until the UI change is rolled out. Will open PR for revert.
Raj Patel [2026-03-11T09:50Z]: Manual backfills complete for current affected users. Deploying revert PR to staging for verification.

Security (Liam Nguyen) [2026-03-11T10:30Z]: Manual backfill entries have been recorded in audit trail. Please ensure any revert doesn't reintroduce the deprecated role creation path without owner approval.

source.summary: CI Terraform run blocked: CMEK/KMS keyRing and key creation denied by org-policy during dedicated-prod onboarding
source.description:
Issue summary:
CI (terraform) provisioning step that creates a KMS KeyRing and CMEK key in project redwood-prod-dedicated failed with org-policy DENY on keyRing.create and serviceAccount.impersonate. This blocks onboarding of analytics-replicator-sa for Dedicated environment and prevents terraform apply from completing.

Impact:
- New Dedicated capacity onboarding is blocked (analytics-replicator service) delaying customer migration.
- CI pipeline failing on every run; engineers cannot complete infra changes requiring CMEK.
- Risk of manual key creation and out-of-band grants if not resolved quickly.

Environment:
- GCP project: redwood-prod-dedicated
- CI: github-actions self-hosted runners in prod-us-west
- Terraform version: 1.6.8; google provider: 4.79.0
- Affected regions: us-west1 (primary), us-east1 (replica)

Steps to reproduce:
1. Checkout branch infra/cmek/onboard-analytics
2. Trigger CI workflow .github/workflows/provision-cmek.yaml or run `terraform apply` with service account impersonation from the runner.
3. Observe error during `google_kms_key_ring` creation step.

Observed logs / errors:
- iam.serviceAccounts.signBlob denied for impersonation attempts (logs attached in CI run).
- Error: "googleapi: Error 403: Permission 'resourcemanager.projects.create' denied by organization policy 'constraints/iam.disableServiceAccountKeyCreation'" (note: message varies depending on provider call).
- Audit log excerpt: orgpolicy.v1 DenyViolation on projects/redwood-prod-dedicated: constraint=constraints/gcp.restrictResourceCreation, denied_action=cloudkms.keyRings.create

Investigation notes:
- Org policy audit shows a recently-applied policy (GCP Org Policy: restrictResourceCreation -> permittedResources limited to team-managed projects) that blocks programmatic creation of KMS KeyRings unless the request originates from a whitelisted principal.
- CI runner uses impersonation of ci-service-sa to perform terraform; the org-policy expects either an approved infra-service account or a signed approval tag on the request.
- Cross-referenced with INT-572913 which flagged a similar deny pattern for a different project; that ticket included an org-policy change by Security on 2026-03-01.
- Hypothesis: Security applied a tightened policy during the compliance sweep; Dedicated onboarding path did not get the required exception.

Temporary mitigation / workaround:
- Manual creation: Infra can manually create the KeyRing and CMEK via an owner account and then grant the required roles to analytics-replicator-sa. This is sensitive and requires audit entry and rotation plan.
- Alternative: run Terraform with a privileged admin service account (not recommended long-term) and then reduce privileges after provisioning.

Proposed remediation steps:
1. Confirm with Security whether the org-policy change on 2026-03-01 is intentional for all projects or scoped to org-folder.
2. If intentional, request a scoped exception for projects/redwood-prod-dedicated for the resource types cloudkms.keyRings.create and cloudkms.cryptoKeys.create for the infra provisioning principal (ci-provisioner-sa) for 48 hours to allow onboarding.
3. Update infra onboarding docs to include the required pre-created resources and the approved SA impersonation list.
4. Post-fix: run terraform apply from CI and validate audit logs show the provisioning principal and not a human owner.
5. If exception is not approved, plan documented manual provisioning runbook and AM/PM rotation for keys.

Next steps / owners:
- Reach out to Security (tag: @security-org-policies) to validate policy intent and request exception.
- Infra/Platform (assignee Priya Natarajan) to prepare justification and minimum-scope exception request and to test manual create steps if required.
- Compliance to log the manual intervention if chosen.

SLA notes:
- Customer impact: internal onboarding delay only, but flagged P1 due to pipeline-blocking nature.
- First response due by: 2026-03-10 (same day triage).
comments: 2026-03-10 Marco Lin (Reporter): Created ticket after CI run failed in prod-us-west. Included CI run id: 11234 and audit log excerpt. Marking P1 due to blocked provisioning.

2026-03-11 Priya Natarajan (Assignee): Began investigation. Confirmed org-policy deny on cloudkms.keyRings.create. Reached out to Security with initial findings and requested confirmation of change on 2026-03-01. Added linked issue INT-572913 for context.

2026-03-11 Sagar Patel (Security): Noted the policy was part of a compliance hardening sweep; default scoping was set too broadly. Requested list of projects and principals that need exception. Suggested short-lived exception via Access Approvals for infra provisioning.

2026-03-11 Priya Natarajan: Uploaded minimal justification bundle and proposed exception scope (projects/redwood-prod-dedicated, principals: ci-provisioner-sa, 48h window). Also documented exact resource URIs and terraform resource names for the Security review.

2026-03-12 Marco Lin: Performed manual create walkthrough with Infra owner to unblock urgent customer onboarding; documented steps and audit entries in confluence. This is temporary and pending Security exception. Added follow-up task to rotate keys and remove manual grants after automation is verified.

2026-03-12 Sagar Patel (Security): Approved a narrow, timebound Access Approval: exception granted for 48 hours to allow provisioning; Security will validate audit logs post-provisioning and then revoke exception.

source.summary: Phased access ramp for contractor->employee conversion with device entitlement hold
source.description:
Issue summary:
We have an external contractor converting to full-time employee (start date 2026-03-22). HR requests a phased access ramp: maintain contractor service accounts for a 2-week overlap but disable privileged groups until completion of background check and I-9. Device entitlement hold: current loaner MacBook must be retained by IT until device transfer checklist completed.

Impact:
Potential gap in access for cross-team handover; risk of orphaned service credentials if not rotated timely.

Context and constraints:
- Contractor uses several service accounts used in CI and internal datasets.
- Payroll/vendor sync must run prior to SSO provisioning for benefits.
- Need to ensure disk encryption keys and KMS access are transferred or rekeyed.

Requested changes:
1) Create bridge account (ephemeral) for two-week overlap with read-only access to production logs.
2) Schedule KMS rekey and rotate service-account keys during off-hours (window requested 2026-03-24 02:00-04:00 UTC).
3) Hold device entitlement: IT retains loaner laptop until hardware transfer checklist signed; create ticket for device reimage and company asset tag update.
4) Delay inclusion in 'privileged' LDAP groups until HR onboarding complete and manager confirms.
5) Produce short playbook for SRE and Security for this conversion flow.

Steps to reproduce/validate:
- HR confirms conversion in Greenhouse -> triggers SCIM event expected.
- Verify that bridge account is created with MFA enforced.
- Run KMS key rotation plan in staging, validate app connectivity.

Attachments:
- Offer letter redacted (link withheld)
- Current service account names: svc-ci-build, svc-data-sync (provided separately to SRE)

Notes:
This is time-sensitive to avoid payroll and benefits delays. Security requests minimal exposure during overlap.
comments: 2026-03-10 11:02 - Jordan Lee (IT): Acknowledged. I'll prepare an ephemeral bridge account template and list of service accounts we need rotated. Need HR confirmation of exact last contractor day.
2026-03-10 15:18 - Priya Kapoor (HR): Confirmed conversion. Contractor last billed day 2026-03-21; official start 2026-03-22. Payroll sync scheduled for 2026-03-21 08:00 UTC.
2026-03-11 09:45 - Omar Ruiz (SRE): KMS rekey window ok. Will run in maintenance window 2026-03-24 02:00-04:00 UTC; will exercise rollback plan in staging first.
2026-03-11 14:22 - Leila Morgan (Security): We need to ensure bridge account has no token issuance rights and is excluded from audit-exempt groups. Also request a short runbook for the rotation.

investigation_notes: Reviewed SCIM logs: last provisioning event for contractor visible on 2026-01-08. SCIM webhook appears healthy.
Service account dependency graph generated showing svc-data-sync used by nightly ETL; rotation needs coordinated downtime or fallback.
Confirmed no active console sessions for the contractor outside office hours; MFA present.
KMS policy attached to svc-data-sync allows rotation by infra-admins; need to add rotation role to runbook.
source.resolution: Pending: bridge account template created and staged; KMS rotation scheduled; device hold staged for pickup. Finalize playbook and confirm privileges removal after HR signal.

KMS rekey runbook draft in Confluence awaiting Security review.
Pickup scheduled with IT logistics for 2026-03-22; device will be reimaged and asset tag updated to ATH-4482.

title: Phased access ramp for contractor->employee conversion with device entitlement hold
preview:
Issue summary:
We have an external contractor converting to full-time employee (start date 2026-03-22). HR requests a phased access ramp: maintain contractor service accounts for a 2-week overlap but disable privileged groups until completion of background check and I-9. Device entitlement hold: current loaner MacBook must be retained by IT until device transfer checklist completed.

Impact:
Potential gap in access for cross-team handover; risk of orphaned service credentials if not rotated timely.

Context and constraints:
- Contractor uses several service accounts used in CI and internal datasets.
- Payroll/vendor sync must run prior to SSO provisioning for benefits.
- Need to ensure disk encryption keys and KMS access are transferred or rekeyed.

Requested changes:
1) Create bridge account (ephemeral) for two-week overlap with read-only access to production logs.
2) Schedule KMS rekey and rotate service-account keys during off-hours (window requested 2026-03-24 02:00-04:00 UTC).
3) Hold device entitlement: IT retains loaner laptop until hardware transfer checklist signed; create ticket for device reimage and company asset tag update.
4) Delay inclusion in 'privileged' LDAP groups until HR onboarding complete and manager confirms.
5) Produce short playbook for SRE and Security for this conversion flow.

Steps to reproduce/validate:
- HR confirms conversion in Greenhouse -> triggers SCIM event expected.
- Verify that bridge account is created with MFA enforced.
- Run KMS key rotation plan in staging, validate app connectivity.

Attachments:
- Offer letter redacted (link withheld)
- Current service account names: svc-ci-build, svc-data-sync (provided separately to SRE)

Notes:
This is time-sensitive to avoid payroll and benefits delays. Security requests minimal exposure during overlap.

source.summary: After-hours staging room lockout during scheduled desk relocation - batch asset scan incomplete, temporary badge reissue and escorted retrieval required
source.description: Issue summary: On 2026-03-10 during an after-hours desk relocation, the staging room (Floor 5, West wing) auto-locked while IT was mid-inventory. Impact: 28 devices (laptops, docking stations, and monitors) had incomplete barcode scans; several devices became inaccessible behind the locked door, preventing finish of asset transfer to storage. Location: Building 3 - Floor 5 West - Staging Room B. Steps to reproduce: 1) Schedule desk move outside of core hours. 2) Move assets to Staging Room B. 3) Attempt to finish batch scan when door controller triggers auto-lock due to scheduled lock at 19:00. Expected: Batch scan completes and assets are moved to storage with badges reactivated as needed. Actual: Door locked at 19:01 due to controller clock drift; scanner session terminated and 9 devices remained unscanned and behind locked door. Operational impact: Delayed onboarding for 3 hires, potential missing asset records until reconciliation.
steps_to_reproduce: Schedule desk move outside business hours
Move equipment into Staging Room B
Attempt batch barcode scan after 18:50
Observe door controller scheduled lock at 19:00 which triggers at 19:01 due to drift
Scanner session ends and room is inaccessible
comments: 2026-03-10 19:18 - Ava Chen (IT Ops): Noted auto-lock occurred during move; 9 items unscanned. Request emergency badge and escort to retrieve devices. Slack thread #facilities-staging referenced: https://slack.redwood.com/archives/CFAC12345
2026-03-10 19:42 - Marco Li (Facilities): Acknowledged. Requested security escort. Opened access-ticket to door-controller vendor (vendor ticket VND-5567).

2026-03-10 20:05 - Priya Patel (Security): Escort assigned (Officer Ramos). Temporary badge generated and assigned to Officer Ramos for single-entry window. Security logs show door unlocked at 20:12 for 10 minutes.

2026-03-10 21:00 - Diego Martinez (Asset Mgmt): Conducted retrieval with Officer Ramos. Scanned 9 previously unscanned assets; 2 mismatched serials flagged for follow-up. Uploaded interim CSV: https://files.redwood.com/internal/asset-recon/INT-94612-interim.csv
2026-03-11 09:12 - Marco Li (Facilities): Replaced door controller battery and resynchronized controller clock to NTP. Vendor VND-5567 updated; no firmware changes needed.
2026-03-12 11:30 - Ava Chen (IT Ops): Completed full inventory reconciliation; updated asset records and closed ticket. Recommended update to desk-move runbook to include pre-lock check.
investigation_notes: Door controller event log: scheduled lock at 19:00 triggered at 19:01 due to 61s clock drift on controller (last NTP sync 2026-02-10).
Scanner (Zebra MC3300) session terminated with HTTP 408 to asset API when token expired during lock; scanner app version 3.4.1.
Asset CSV showed two serials (QX1234, QX1235) that did not match expected device IDs — traced to previous move bundle; both devices located and tags updated.
Security access logs correlate with temporary badge ID TMP-3742 assigned to Officer Ramos at 20:08 and used for single-entry at 20:12.
source.resolution_summary: Temporary badge issued to Security escort; assets retrieved 20:12-20:22; full inventory reconciliation completed 2026-03-12. Facilities replaced door controller battery, resynced NTP, and scheduled quarterly NTP checks. IT updated scanner token refresh to 12h (from 8h) as a mitigating change to reduce mid-scan auth expirations. Runbook action: Added pre-lock check and explicit 'lock-window' hold for after-hours moves. Follow-ups: 1) Facilities to add NTP check to monthly facility checklist (owner: Marco Li). 2) Asset Management to run a cross-check script for moves over 10 devices (owner: Diego Martinez).

source.summary: Office AP power-save / DHCP rapid-renew causes WireGuard handshake to stall and internal service blackholes
source.description: Multiple employees reported intermittent loss of internal services and failed WireGuard handshakes when switching between personal hotspot and corporate Wi‑Fi. Symptoms include long pauses during ssh/kshell connect, stalled LLM inference calls from internal tooling, and zero traffic observed through the WireGuard peer despite tunnel remaining logically up.
steps_to_reproduce: Connect MacBook (macOS 13.x Intel + Thunderbolt dock) to cellular hotspot and start an active WireGuard session to corporate gateway.
Switch to corporate SSID (802.11ax enterprise) and roam between two nearby APs (manual roam trigger or walk between desks).
Observe WireGuard handshake stalls, KV-cache-backed inference RPCs drop or hang, and internal services report client IP mismatch.
Check client and firewall logs for UDP flow entries and DHCP lease events.
logs: Client tcpdump excerpt: '14:23:02.123 IP 10.52.1.82.51820 > gw.example.net.51820: UDP, length 1200' followed by '14:23:02.456 IP 10.52.1.82.51820 > gw.example.net.51820: UDP, length 0 (encap)' then no replies for ~30s.
DHCP server log: 'DHCPDISCOVER from 84:2b:14:... rapid-renew, offering same IP 10.52.1.82', 'DHCPACK issued with lease-time 600s, renew requested immediately'
Branch firewall anti-spoof log: 'SPOOF-DROP: interface br0: src 10.52.1.82 seen on port AP3 then port AP7 within 3s' (rule 3104)
Zero-trust agent logs on client: 'network-change detected: ifindex 5->7, rebind failed: socket busy'
investigation_notes: Hypothesis 1: AP power-save and aggressive client roaming cause very short DHCP renews / rapid rebindings; firewall anti-spoof rules consider the quick IP appearance on different APs as duplicate/malicious and drop UDP.

Hypothesis 2: Endpoint zero-trust agent doesn't gracefully rebind WireGuard socket on interface change when Thunderbolt dock interfaces reorder, causing in-transit UDP packets to be dropped by downstream NAT.
Packet captures show client continues to send large UDP packets without receiving responses; firewall logs show drops due to 'source-on-different-port' enforcement.
Replication: reproduced on macOS Intel and one Windows 11 laptop with vendor NIC driver 22.30; did not reproduce on Android hotspot clients.
workarounds: On APs: disable aggressive client power-save / enable low-latency roaming mode for affected AP group.
On firewall: temporarily relax anti-spoof threshold to allow 5s grace period for same src IP on different AP ports (rule 3104 -> 3112 emulation).
On endpoints: instruct affected users to toggle Wi‑Fi off before switching networks to force clean rebind.
Endpoint team to push updated zero-trust agent that explicitly closes and recreates WireGuard socket on network-change event.
action_items: Network: Update AP group profile to 802.11k/minimize power-save timer, and schedule config change for branch floor A (ETA 2026-03-15).
Security: Patch firewall rule to add short grace window for DHCP-renew related IP movements (owner: Priya Shah).
Endpoint: Add network-change rebind logic to zero-trust agent PR (see related_github_prs) and QA across macOS/Windows.
SRE: Add dashboard panel highlighting 'WireGuard handshake failure rate' by AP and client MAC for 24h window.
comments: Ava Chen (Reporter) 2026-03-10: 'Received three Slack reports in #it-support within an hour. All impacted users were near AP cluster A3-A7.'

Marco Ruiz (Assignee) 2026-03-11: 'Confirmed reproducible on my test MacBook with Thunderbolt dock attached. Captured pcap and firewall logs, uploaded to S3: s3://redwood-incident-data/INT-991000/pcaps.tar.gz'
Priya Shah (Firewall) 2026-03-11: 'We can relax the anti-spoof rule for the AP VLAN for up to 10 minutes, but prefer a targeted 5s window. Will coordinate maintenance window.'
Endpoint Team 2026-03-12: 'We can land a rebind patch to the agent but need two days of QA; will provide an alpha build for affected users tomorrow.'
